clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥60 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell lineage or T‑cell lineage. Cancer burden allowed: Previously untreated disease with bone‐marrow involvement ≥20 % lymphoblasts; no prior systemic antileukemia therapy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Venetoclax exposure. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of Philadelphia chromosome/t(9;22) BCR‑ABL fusion **or** morphological features of Burkitt‑type leukemia/lymphoma **or** diagnosis of lymphoblastic lymphoma instead of leukemia.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonia/pneumonitis,
Uncontrolled infections,
Active hepatitis B or C infection,
Human immunodeficiency virus infection,
Pregnancy or lactation,
Recent major surgery or radiotherapy within four weeks,
Symptomatic or untreated leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression,
Severe cardiovascular disease including New York Heart Association Class III–IV, recent myocardial infarction, stroke, or unstable angina within six months, or left ventricular ejection fraction < 40 %,
Concurrent use of anticoagulant warfarin,
Significant malabsorption syndromes preventing oral intake,
Any other serious acute or chronic medical or psychiatric disorder deemed unsuitable by the investigators.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell lineage or T‑cell lineage. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after receipt of ≥1 cytotoxic‑containing chemotherapy regimen, with bone‑marrow involvement ≥5 % lymphoblasts. Prior treatment required: At least one prior cytotoxic chemotherapy regimen. Prior treatment excluded: Venetoclax exposure. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of Philadelphia chromosome/t(9;22) BCR‑ABL fusion **or** morphological features of Burkitt‑type leukemia/lymphoma **or** diagnosis of lymphoblastic lymphoma instead of leukemia.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified; high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements; high‑grade B‑cell lymphoma not otherwise specified; primary mediastinal (thymic) large B‑cell lymphoma; transformed lymphoma (e.g., transformed follicular lymphoma, marginal zone lymphoma, follicular lymphoma grade 3). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after failure of ≥2 lines of systemic chemotherapy—including an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody and an anthracycline—and either progression after autologous stem cell transplantation or ineligibility/not proceeding to transplant. Prior treatment required: At minimum two prior systemic regimens that contained rituximab (or equivalent anti‑CD20 agent) and an anthracycline-containing backbone; optional prior autologous stem cell transplant permitted. Prior treatment excluded: Prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy for any indication; prior allogeneic stem cell transplant; prior bispecific T‑cell engager antibodies; prior T‑cell receptor‑engineered cellular therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Inability to give informed consent  
* Current HIV infection or active hepatitis B surface antigen positivity; untreated/hepatitis C viremia  
* Pharmacologically uncontrolled seizures  
* Autoimmune central nervous system disorders (multiple sclerosis, optic neuritis, etc.)  
* Other CNS conditions judged to impede neurotoxicty assessment (midline shift on MRI, abnormal CSF opening pressure or CSF protein >150 mg/dL)  
* Recent (<3 months) whole-brain radiation therapy  
* Active systemic fungal, viral, or bacterial infection  
* Pregnancy or breastfeeding  
* Prior/concurrent malignant neoplasm outside approved exceptions (except adequately treated skin carcinomas, in‑situ cervical or breast carcinoma, hormone‑treated breast/prostate cancer in remission ≥2 years, or fully resected solid tumors in remission ≥2 years)  
* Severe immunocompromise from ongoing treatment of non‑neurological autoimmune disease  
* Requirement for chronic systemic steroids ≧10 mg prednisolone daily (≤2 mg dexamethasone allowable for CNS cohort)  
* Significant cardiovascular disease within past 6 months (myocardial infarction, coronary intervention, unstable angina)  
* Concomitant radiotherapy (palliative normal‑tissue‑sparring acceptable; therapeutic radiotherapy must end ≥2 weeks before leukapheresis)  
* Baseline dementia interfering with assessments (per ICE score)  
* History of serious immediate hypersensitivity to any investigational product components  
* Declination to partake in long‑term follow‑up lentiviral gene‑therapy protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: B‑cell primary central nervous system lymphoma or secondary central nervous system lymphoma. Histology allowed: B‑cell lymphoma confined to the central nervous system (either de novo primary CNS lymphoma or secondary CNS involvement of systemic B‑cell NHL). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease after failing at least first‑line therapy for primary CNS lymphoma, or after ≥1 prior systemic line for secondary CNS lymphoma. Prior treatment required: Receipt of at least one appropriate frontline regimen for CNS lymphoma (typically methotrexate‑based) and, where applicable, prior systemic therapy that incorporated an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody and an anthracycline; prior autologous stem cell transplant permissible but not mandatory. Prior treatment excluded: Same global exclusions as Space 1 (CAR‑T therapy, allogeneic transplant, bispecific antibodies, TCR‑engineered therapy). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Mantle cell lymphoma. Prior treatment required: One prior cytotoxic rituximab‑based chemotherapy regimen (examples: rituximab‑bendamustine, R‑CHOP, R‑DHAP, R‑ara‑C) **and** exposure to a Bruton's Tyrosine Kinase inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: Same global exclusions as Spaces 1‑2 (CAR‑T therapy, allogeneic transplant, bispecific antibodies, TCR‑engineered therapy). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Prior treatment excluded: Same global exclusions as preceding spaces (CAR‑T therapy, allogeneic transplant, bispecific antibodies, TCR‑engineered therapy). Biomarkers required: NA. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: Any ovarian cancer histology. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced disease that is platinum‑refractory or platinum‑resistant with measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1. Prior treatment required: Prior receipt of platinum‑containing chemotherapy leading to refractory or resistant disease. Prior treatment excluded: Antibody‑drug conjugate with a topoisomerase I payload. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active or progressing brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease; stable-treated brain metastases meeting predefined stability criteria are permissible. Persistent (>grade 1) toxicities from prior systemic anticancer therapy (except alopecia and vitiligo). Recent extensive radiotherapy or focal palliation near target lesions. Major surgery within four weeks lacking adequate recovery. Clinically significant underlying lung disease requiring systemic steroids within six months. Current acute/chronic pancreatitis or cirrhosis exceeding Child‑Pugh A. Recent hepatic decompensation (jaundice, encephalopathy, variceal bleeding) within sixty days. History of liver transplant or allogeneic stem‑cell transplantation. Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (recent MI, NYHA Class III-IV CHF, uncontrolled HTN, serious arrhythmia, severe valvular disease). Thromboembolic or cerebrovascular event within three months. Active untreated infectious illnesses—including bacterial, fungal, viral infections such as hepatitis B/C or HIV (unless controlled per protocol specifications). Known hypersensitivity to study drug components. Participation in another investigational trial concurrently. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Prior malignant neoplasm (aside from index cancer) within three years, except appropriately treated non‑melanoma skin cancers, superficial bladder cancer, in‑situ cervix/breast/prostate carcinomas with no activity for two years. Any other severe acute or chronic medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormalities judged unsuitable for enrolment.

Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of standard systemic anticancer therapy appropriate for the tumor type. Prior treatment excluded: Antibody‑drug conjugate with a topoisomerase I payload. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or locally recurrent unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Documentation of radiographic or objective progression on a cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor‐containing regimen in the metastatic setting **or** relapse/progression occurring during or within 12 months of completing such a regimen in the adjuvant setting, and documentation of progression on fulvestrant (as monotherapy or in combination) in the metastatic setting; no more than two prior chemotherapy regimens in the metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to any MEK inhibitor; prior discontinuation of abemaciclib for toxicity; prior discontinuation of fulvestrant for toxicity. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive **or** progesterone receptor positive **and** human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease (stable treated brain metastases permissible).
Pre‑existing ocular pathology increasing risk for retinal vascular events or other serious eye conditions.
History of rhabdomyolysis or neuromuscular disorders associated with elevated creatine kinase.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses including, but not limited to, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, New York Heart Association class III–IV heart failure, recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, symptomatic coronary artery disease, uncontrolled diabetes mellitus, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, severe chronic liver disease, severe renal impairment, or any condition placing the patient at unacceptable risk for toxicity.
Second malignant neoplasm unless disease free ≥5 years (except adequately treated cervix carcinoma in situ or non‑melanoma skin cancer) or meets stipulated exception criteria.
Known hypersensitivity to components of VS‑6766, abemaciclib, fulvestrant, or excipients thereof.
Documented HIV infection with an AIDS‑defining opportunistic infection within the past 12 months or receiving potent CYP3A4‑modulating antiretrovirals.
Active hepatitis B virus infection (surface antigen positive) or active hepatitis C virus infection not controlled per protocol specifications.
Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraception compliance.
Incapacity to ingest/swallow oral medication or conditions impairing gastrointestinal absorption (gastrectomy, extensive small‑bowel resections, active inflammatory bowel disease, baseline diarrhoea ≥Grade 2, etc.).
Active systemic bacterial or fungal infection requiring intravenous antimicrobial therapy, or SARS‑CoV‑2 infection ≤28 days prior to first dose.
Concurrent use of warfarin (requirement to convert to alternative anticoagulation).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: essential thrombocythemia. Histology allowed: essential thrombocythemia (myeloid proliferative neoplasm). Cancer burden allowed: Low‑risk or intermediate‑risk disease per International Prognostic Score for Essential Thrombocytosis (IPSET)—defined as no history of thrombosis and either age < 60 years (regardless of JAK2 mutation status) or age > 60 years with wild‑type JAK2. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Concurrent investigational agents; Potent cytochrome P450 3A4–inhibiting medicines. Biomarkers required: Diagnosis verified by World Health Organization 2016 criteria. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: polycythemia vera. Histology allowed: polycythemia vera (myeloid proliferative neoplasm). Cancer burden allowed: Low‑risk disease per National Comprehensive Cancer Network guidelines—characterized by no history of thrombosis and age < 60 years. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Concurrent investigational agents; Potent cytochrome P450 3A4–inhibiting medicines. Biomarkers required: Diagnosis verified by World Health Organization 2016 criteria. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: renally originated carcinoma. Histology allowed: clear cell renal cell carcinoma **or** translocation renal cell carcinoma **or** chromophobe renal cell carcinoma (rhabdoid or sarcomatoid features permissible if a qualifying component is present). Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease with measurable lesions per standard radiologic criteria. Prior treatment required: progression after at least one US Food and Drug Administration‑approved programmed death‑protein 1/programmed death ligand‐1 inhibitor **and** exposure to at least one vascular endothelial growth factor receptor tyrosine kinase inhibitor (or documented contraindication to such inhibitors). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: urothelial carcinoma. Histology allowed: any urothelial carcinoma histology (presence of rhabdoid or sarcomatoid differentiation allowable if urothelial component remains). Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease with measurable lesions per standard radiologic criteria. Prior treatment required: progression after at least one US Food and Drug Administration‑approved programmed death‑protein 1/programmed death ligand‑1 inhibitor **and** failure of at least one cytotoxic chemotherapeutic regimen **or** an antibody‑ drug conjugate directed against urothelial carcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and female. Cancer type allowed: Advanced solid tumor (unresectable or metastatic). Histology allowed: Any. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable disease per RECIST v1.1; documented radiographic progression after most recent therapy; advanced/unresectable or metastatic disease; patients with asymptomatic, radiographically stable central nervous system metastases off steroid (>10 mg prednisolone daily) are permissible. Prior treatment required: Disease relapse or progression after ≥1 prior standard systemic anticancer therapy (or intolerance/to lack of approved option). Prior treatment excluded: Prior HER3‑directed therapy; prior exposure to an antibody‑drug conjugate containing a topoisomerase I inhibitor (exceptions confined to specific investigational cohorts). Biomarkers required: Provision of archived or fresh tumor tissue suitable for assessment of HER3 expression (to be measured during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: None

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: conventional chondrosarcoma. Histology allowed: conventional chondrosarcoma Grade 1 or Grade 2 or Grade 3. Cancer burden allowed: locally advanced or metastatic disease not suitable for curative resection with at least one measurable lesion per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior idh1 inhibitor therapy; receipt of more than one prior systemic antitumour regimen in the advanced/metastatic setting. Biomarkers required: idh1 mutation (R132C or R132L or R132G or R132H or R132S) identified by central laboratory testing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable to swallow oral medication.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Participation in another interventional study concurrently.
Systemic anticancer therapy less than 2 weeks prior to randomisation (investigational or immune‑based therapy less than 4 weeks).
Radiotherapy less than 2 weeks prior to randomisation.
Symptomatic brain metastases requiring steroid dose greater than 10 mg prednisolone equivalents per day.
History of another primary malignancy except for cured non‑melanoma skin cancer, cured carcinoma in situ, pT1‑2 prostate cancer Gleason ≤6, or being disease‑free for ≥1 year.
Major surgery within 4 weeks preceding randomisation.
Significant active cardiovascular disease within 6 months, including NYHA class III/IV heart failure, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, or stroke.
Left ventricular ejection fraction lower than 40%.
Corrected QT interval (Fridericia) equal to or exceeding 450 mseconds or other risks for QT prolongation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: gastrointestinal stromal tumor. Histology allowed: gastrointestinal stromal tumor. Cancer burden allowed: advanced (locally unresectable or metastatic) disease with radiographic progression after imatinib. Prior treatment required: imatinib (with documented disease progression). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: KIT exon 11 mutation and (KIT exon 17 mutation or KIT exon 18 mutation) identified by circulating tumor DNA assay; testing performed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: KIT exon 9 mutation, KIT exon 13 mutation, KIT exon 14 mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of active central nervous system metastases.; New York Heart Association Class II‑IV heart disease, myocardial infarction within six months, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias, uncontrolled hypertension, or congestive heart failure.; Use of strong or moderate cyclooxygenase‑P450 3A inhibitors or inducers, or ingestion of grapefruit/grapefruit juice within two weeks preceding dosing.; Major surgical procedures (e.g., abdominal laparotomy) within four weeks prior to first dose.; Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection or hepatitis B/C infection when receiving prohibited concomitant medications.; Inability to swallow oral medication, malabsorptive conditions, or dependence on parenteral nutrition.; Ongoing active non‑procedural bleeding (excluding minor hemorrhage such as hemorrhoids or gingival bleedings).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: solid tumors (any anatomical origin, including primary brain tumor). Histology allowed: any solid tumor histology. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: documented disease progression after the most recent anti-cancer therapy and no satisfactory alternative therapeutic option. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history or current evidence of retinal vein occlusion; concurrent neuromuscular disorder associated with elevated creatine kinase; brain metastasis larger than 4 centimeters.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: solid tumors (excluding melanoma) that are unresectable or advanced/metastatic. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Endometrial cancer. Histology allowed: Endometrial carcinoma. Uncontrolled brain metastases requiring corticosteroids, surgery, or radiation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Known allergy to hyaluronic acid.
Pathologic T4 disease.
Inflammatory bowel disease requiring steroid treatment (Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis).
Active connective tissue disorder including lupus or scleroderma.
Any urogenital abnormality that could limit access to the Barrigel injection site.
White blood cell count <4000/μL or >12,000/μL.
Hemoglobin <10 g/dL (transfusion permitted to meet threshold).
Active bleeding disorder or clinically significant coagulopathy (PTT >35 seconds, INR >1.4, platelets <100,000/mm³).
Serum AST/ALT >2.5× ULN.
Creatinine >2.0 mg/dL.
Bilirubin >2.0 mg/dL.
History of chronic renal failure.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (fasting glucose >300 mg/dL or symptomatic hyperglycemia despite medication).
Acquired immune deficiency syndrome (history of AIDS); controlled HIV infection allowable if CD4⁺ ≥350 cells/µL and no AIDS‑defining illnesses.
Contraindication to magnetic resonance imaging or PSMA/F^18–fluciclovine PET scanning (e.g., non‑MRI‑compatible devices).
Presence of bilateral hip implants (unilateral MRI‑compatible implant permissible if artifacts do not interfere).
Unable or unwilling to comply with study requirements.
Any condition deemed by the investigator to preclude safe administration or completion of study therapy.

Pregnancy, lactation, or intent to become pregnant.

Chronic inflammatory bowel disease flare or recent GI perforation. Ongoing serious infections requiring antimicrobial therapy. Clinically significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin > upper limits, transaminases markedly elevated except as permitted). Renal impairment (glomerular filtration rate <30 ml/min/1.73 m²). Poor performance status (ECOG > 1). Concurrent enrollment in another therapeutic clinical trial or receipt of other investigational agents. Recent (<4 weeks) cytotoxic chemotherapy, targeted therapy, radiotherapy, or biological therapy (excluding stipulated washout windows). Known hypersensitivity to components of sacituzumab govitecan, enfortumab vedotin, pembrolizumab, or their excipients. Autoimmune diseases requiring systemic immunosuppression within the past 2 years. High-dose glucocorticoid use (>10 mg prednisolone daily) or other potent immunosuppressants. Uncontrolled diabetes (HbA1c > 8%). Untreated/uncontrolled hypertension or electrolyte abnormalities. Pregnancy or lactation. Lack of reliable contraceptive measures for individuals of reproductive potential. Any concomitant medical or psychiatric condition judged by investigator to interfere with safe conduct of the study or accurate data collection.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Other invasive malignant neoplasm diagnosed within the preceding five years, except treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer or remote (>5 years) breast cancer treated with adjuvant chemotherapy.  
Synchronous primary invasive ovarian or cervical cancer.  
Active or historical autoimmune disease or documented immune deficiency.  
History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organising pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or current pneumonitis evident on baseline thoracic imaging.  
Active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.  
Severe infectious episode occurring within four weeks before screening.  
Significant underlying cardiovascular disease.  
Administration of a live attenuated vaccine within four weeks prior to enrolment or anticipated need for such a vaccine during the study.  
Previous allogenic hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation or solid‑organ transplantation.  
Untreated central nervous system involvement, uncontrolled seizure disorder, or clinically apparent stroke/transient ischemic attack/subarachnoid haemorrhage within six months.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Mucosal melanoma. Histology allowed: Mucosal melanoma. general_exclusion_criteria: No history of acute coronary syndrome, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary artery bypass graft, coronary angioplasty, or stent placement within 6 months. No current Class II or higher congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association). No refractory hypertension (systolic > 140 mm Hg or diastolic > 90 mm Hg despite medication). No history of myocarditis, syncope of cardiac etiology, uncontrolled arrhythmias, high-grade atrioventricular block without pacing, or cerebrovascular accident/TIA within 3 months. No prolonged corrected QT interval (>500 ms). No congenital bleeding disorders, recent GI bleed needing intervention (<6 months), active hemoptysis (<42 days), cavernous pulmonary lesions, vascular invasion by tumor, active thrombotic event requiring therapeutic anticoagulation, unexplained cytopenias lasting >3 months, myelodysplastic syndrome, or other hematologic malignancy. No active bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring treatment at baseline. No gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption. No active autoimmune disease requiring systemic glucocorticoids >10 mg/day prednisolone equivalent or other immunosuppression within 14 days. No autoimmune neurologic disease (Guillain‑Barré, Myasthenia Gravis) or prior non‑infectious pneumonitis. No severe allergy to study agents or their constituents. No history of gastrointestinal perforation or intra‑abdominal fistula. No clinically evident leptomeningeal or parenchymal CNS metastases unless previously treated, stable off therapy ≥28 days, and asymptomatic. No seizures or conditions increasing seizure risk within 2 years. No Child‑Pugh class B/C cirrhosis, portal hypertensive complications, or severe hepatic insufficiency. No untreated spinal cord compression or imminent vertebral collapse; spine disease must be stabilized before enrolment. No chronic strong cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme modulators (inducers or inhibitors) taken concurrently.

Severe concomitant illness or laboratory abnormalities that would compromise safety or interpretation of study outcomes.

general_exclusion_criteria: Female participants who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or plan to become pregnant or breast‑feed while enrolled. Participation prohibited for individuals with central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Positive human immunodeficiency virus infection. Active hepatitis B virus infection (positive HBsAg or HBV DNA+) or active hepatitis C virus infection (detectable HCV RNA). Current systemic immunosuppressive therapy exceeding low‑dose corticosteroids (<20 mg/day prednisone equivalent permissible). Active or unresolved autoimmune haemolytic anaemia or immune thrombocytopaenia. Use of therapeutic warfarin or warfarin derivatives. Receipt of live, attenuated vaccination within four weeks preceding first study drug dose. Uncontrolled acute bacterial, viral, fungal or parasitic infection (excluding superficial fungal nail infection). Major surgical procedure within four weeks before starting study medication. Significant bleeding diatheses (hemophilia, recurrent spontaneous bleeds needing transfusion). Stroke or intracranial haemorrhage within six months prior to enrolment. Inability to ingest oral capsules. Anticipated inability to attend scheduled outpatient visits and follow‑up assessments. Clinically meaningful untreated cardiovascular disease—including unstable angina within three months, myocardial infarction within six months, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤ 45 % within twelve months, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, or life‑threatening arrhythmias. Documented hypersensitivity to any ingredient of zanubrutinib, bendamustine or rituximab. Non‑compliant behaviour jeopardising safe drug delivery or data integrity. Other malignant solid tumours diagnosed within the past three years, excluding adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, non‑muscle invasive bladder tumour, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia grade 0/I, ductal carcinoma in situ of the breast, or localized Gleason ≤ 6 prostate cancer. Moderate-to-severe underlying pulmonary disease. Ongoing illicit drug/alcohol dependence or unmanaged psychiatric illness impairing study conduct. Concomitant use of potent cytochrome P450 3A enzyme inducers.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma (any invasive subtype). Prior treatment required: Received 1 to 3 prior chemotherapeutic regimens for metastatic breast cancer, **must** include at least one regimen containing an antibody‑drug conjugate; may have received prior immune‑checkpoint inhibitor (PD‑1/L1 blockade); may have received prior platinum‑containing chemotherapy provided no documented disease progression on platinum in the metastatic setting (platinums administered in (neo)adjuvant setting accepted if disease recurred >6 months after finishing platinum). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis.
Unresolved Grade ≥2 toxicity from prior therapies (excluding alopecia).
Active uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (e.g., symptomatic heart failure, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction, uncontrolled hypertension).
Significant ventricular arrhythmias, prolonged QTc (>470 ms), or other clinically important electrocardiographic abnormalities.
Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C) or transaminases markedly elevated outside stipulated limits.
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence.
Untreated or progressive central nervous system metastasis requiring steroids.
Active infection including hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Inability or unwillingness to use appropriate contraception methods.
Concurrent use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inducers that cannot be stopped.
Any other condition judged by the investigator to make safe conduct of the trial unsuitable.

general_exclusion_criteria:
1. Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial.
2. Receipt of other investigational anticancer agents or devices, including carboplatin wafer implants and proteasome inhibitors at recurrence.
3. Prior exposure to procarbazine, lomustine, vincristine (PCV) or any individual component thereof, carmustine wafer implants, or bevacizumab.
4. Prior treatment with poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase (PARP) inhibitors.
5. Major surgery (other than surgery for recurrent diffuse glioma) within four weeks preceding study treatment.
6. Conventional external beam radiotherapy within three months prior to documentation of progression unless progression occurs outside the irradiated volume or is pathologically verified.
7. Prior cranial radiotherapy exceeding 65 Gray, stereotactic radiosurgery, or brachytherapy unless recurrence is histologically proved.
8. Use of full‑dose anticoagulation unless coagulation parameters are therapeutically controlled and dosing has been stable for at least two weeks.
9. Concomitant medication that is a sensitive substrate of CYP2D6 or CYP2C19 and cannot be substituted.
10. Enzyme‑inducing antiepileptic drugs (unless discontinued and replaced with non‑enzyme‑inducing alternatives at least two weeks before enrollment).
11. Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
12. Known hypersensitivity to any component of NMS‑03305293 or temozolomide formulation.
13. Active bacterial, fungal, viral (including HIV) infections requiring systemic therapy.
14. Prolonged corrected QT interval (QTc ≥460 ms in females, ≥450 ms in males) or other risk factors for torsades de pointes.
15. Clinically significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (e.g., ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease, short bowel syndrome).
16. Recent cardiovascular events within six months (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary or peripheral arterial bypass, symptomatic congestive heart failure, stroke, or transient ischemic attack) or active bleeding diathesis.
17. History of another malignant neoplasm within the past three years, excluding adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or localized tumors.
18. Any other severe acute or chronic medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormalities judged by the investigator to pose unacceptable risk or confound outcome assessments.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis. Congestive heart failure or family history of Long QT syndrome. Baseline corrected QT interval >470 ms. Uncontrolled hypertension or significant arrhythmia. Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5× ULN, ALT >3× ULN). Renal impairment (creatinine clearance <70 mL/min/1.73 m²). Poor performance status (Karnofsky <50 for >16 yr old, Lansky <50 for ≤16 yr old). Ongoing severe infectious diseases or uncontrolled systemic illnesses. Human immunodeficiency virus infection, chronic hepatitis B or C infection, or autoimmune disease requiring systemic cytotoxic/immunosuppressive therapy. Current pregnancy or breastfeeding; lack of reliable contraceptive measures for women of child‑bearing potential and men. Recent (<12 months) active secondary malignancy requiring treatment. Participation in another interventional oncology trial involving investigational agents. Receipt of potent CYP3A4/5 inhibitors within 72 hours of study drug start or potent CYP3A4/5 inducers within 2 weeks of study drug start. Evidence of widespread disseminated disease (diffuse leptomeningeal infiltration or cerebrospinal fluid seeding) outside of designated Cohort 5 arms. Allergies to components of ONC201, panobinostat, paxalisib, or associated excipients. Substance misuse or alcohol dependence interfering with compliance.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis. Congestive heart failure or family history of Long QT syndrome. Baseline corrected QT interval >470 ms. Receipt of potent CYP3A4/5 inhibitors within 72 hours of study drug start or potent CYP3A4/5 inducers within 2 weeks of study drug start. Evidence of widespread disseminated disease (diffuse leptomeningeal infiltration or cerebrospinal fluid seeding) outside of designated Cohort 5 arms. Allergies to components of ONC201, panobinostat, paxalisib, or associated excipients. Substance misuse or alcohol dependence interfering with compliance.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer with anticipated time to next systemic therapy <12 months  
• Residual toxicity greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia)  
• Brain metastases  
• Small‑cell carcinoma variant of prostate cancer (small‐cell neuroendocrine pathology)  
• Recent (<6 months) symptomatic skeletal event (spinal cord compression, pathologic fracture, or bone irradiation/surgery)  
• Uncontrolled pain attributable to prostate cancer or comorbidity  
• Rapidly progressing visceral crisis with estimated life expectancy <18 months  
• Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illnesses (cardiac disease NYHA Class III–IV, significant pulmonary disease, infections, etc.)  
• Active second malignant neoplasm  
• Severe sleep disorders (e.g., narcolepsy)  
• Ocular conditions preventing adequate light transmission (untreated cataract, severe glaucoma, macular degeneration, blindness, pupillary abnormalities, retinal disease)  
• Significant psychiatric disturbance (bipolar disorder, mania, severe psychosis)  
• Night‑shift occupational schedule  
• Prior use of light therapy for fatigue/depression  
• Recent (<6 months) intraocular surgery resulting in eye irritation  
• Photosensitivity, seizure disorder, or epilepsy.

Positive serologic testing for hepatitis B surface antigen or hepatitis C antibodies (unless HCV RNA negative). Pregnant or lactating individuals, or anyone unwilling or unable to practice effective contraception throughout the study and for 120 days thereafter. Receipt of therapeutic radiation within ten days prior to initiation of study treatment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Any malignancy presently being treated with an immune checkpoint inhibitor. general_exclusion_criteria:
Colonic perforation or intra‑abdominal abscess.
Partial or complete bowel obstruction within the last three months, symptoms suggestive of obstruction, or imaging showing imminent obstruction.
Active Clostridioides difficile infection or other infectious colitis.
Concomitant hepatitis B virus or hepatitis C virus infection.
History of untreated Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection or positive interferon‑γ release assay without completed prophylactic therapy, and untreated active tuberculoma.
Active or prior infection with nontuberculous mycobacteria.
Unable or unwilling to undergo colonoscopy or flexible sigmoidoscopy.
Requirement for inpatient care at the time of intended treatment administration (subjects must be ambulatory/outpatient).
Previous total proctocolectomy.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant within six months (female participants only).
Inability to provide informed consent.
Recent laboratory‑confirmed SARS‑CoV‑2 infection occurring less than ten days ago for mild cases or twenty days ago for moderate/severe cases prior to first investigational product infusion.
Presence of any medical condition judged by the investigator to place the participant at undue risk when combined with vedolizumab plus abbreviated corticosteroid regimen.
Known hypersensitivity to trimethoprim‑sulfamethoxazole.
Body weight greater than 120 kilograms.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; females of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test ≤7 days before registration.
• Known Gilbert’s syndrome or UGT1A1*28/*28 genotype.
• Baseline Grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy, neurosensory toxicity, or neuromotor toxicity irrespective of cause.
• Active uncontrolled infection or uncontrolled diabetes deemed unsafe by investigator.
• Untreated, symptomatic brain metastases (treated brain metastases allowable under specified stability criteria).
• History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids.
• Prior irreversible immune‑mediated adverse events leading to permanent cessation of immunotherapy.
• Requirement for systemic corticosteroid (>10 mg prednisolone daily) or other immunosuppression within 14 days preceding registration.
• Autoimmune diseases needing systemic therapy within past 6 months (exceptions noted for limited conditions).
• Receipt of allogeneic tissue or organ transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: any. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma (world health organization grade IV idh‑wildtype glioblastoma). Histology allowed: glioblastoma, gliosarcoma, or idh‑wildtype diffuse glioma exhibiting egfr amplification or tert promoter mutation or simultaneous chromosome 7 gain and chromosome 10 loss. Cancer burden allowed: first or second relapse/progressive disease confined to the brain (no leptomeningeal involvement, no extracranial disease). Prior treatment required: receipt of initial radiation therapy with or without temozolomide and up to one additional systemic anticancer therapy leading to first or second relapse. Prior treatment excluded: prior parp inhibitor therapy; prior anti‑vegf targeted therapy (e.g., bevacizumab, cediranib, aflibercept, vandetanib, cabozantinib, sunitinib); prior anti‑pd‑1, pd‑l1, pd‑l2, ctla‑4 or other checkpoint antibody therapy; prior viral therapy or vaccine directed against glioblastoma. Biomarkers required: confirmed idh‑wildtype status (ihc or ngs); presence of egfr amplification **or**tert promoter mutation **or**chromosome 7 gain with chromosome 10 loss (any qualify). Biomarkers excluded: idh mutation; 1p/19q co‑deletion.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of actively progressing secondary malignancy (excluding adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, localized prostate cancer, or cured in‑situ cervix cancer).
Active infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Known HIV infection, active hepatitis B or C infection, or latent tuberculous infection.
Immunodeficiency syndromes, including untreated hiv/AIDS.
Autoimmune disease necessitating systemic immunomodulatory therapy within the preceding 2 years.
Recent myocardial infarction (≤6 months), symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA class III‑IV, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias, structural valve disease, myocarditis, or QTc > 500 ms.
Significant uncontrolled hypertension or peripheral vascular disease.
Severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5×ULN, transaminases >2.5×ULN) or renal impairment (creatinine clearance <50 ml/min).
Clinically meaningful coagulopathies or recent grade ≥ 3 bleeding events.
Unresolved grade ≥ 2 diarrhoea, gastro‑intestinal obstruction, malabsorption, or extensive small‑bowel resection affecting drug absorption.
Major surgery within 4 weeks prior to enrolment or incomplete recovery from prior procedure.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Use of prohibited concomitant medicines (strong cyp3a4 inducers/inhibitors, enzim­e‑inducing anticonvulsants, warfarin/coumarins, live vaccines administered within 30 days, recreational cannabinoids, st john’s wort, kava, ephedra, ginkgo biloba, dhea, yohimbine, saw palmetto, ginseng).

general_exclusion_criteria: History of active primary immunodeficiency, known HIV, active chronic or past hepatitis B infection, or hepatitis C infection.; Uncontrolled intercurrent illness.; History of non‑infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease, current ILD, or suspicion of ILD that cannot be ruled out by imaging at screening.; Severe lung‑specific intercurrent illnesses.; Uncontrolled infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, antivirals, or antifungals.; Presence of pleural effusion, ascites, or pericardial effusion requiring drainage, peritoneal shunt, or CART.; Spinal cord compression or clinically active central nervous system metastases.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease with measurably detectable lesions per Lugano criteria; patient must be ineligible for or have failed autologous stem‑cell transplantation; additionally, patient must have either experienced failure of prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy or be judged ineligible for CAR‑T therapy. Prior treatment required: Failure of at least one prior line of systemic anticancer therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of transformation of indolent disease to diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma; receipt of a solid organ transplant at any time; diagnosed primary mediastinal B‑cell lymphoma; clinically significant active cardiovascular disease or serious cardiac arrhythmia requiring medication; ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease of any grade or treatment thereof; clinically significant pericardial or pleural effusion; ongoing peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 1; history of a second malignant neoplasm unless definitive curative treatment was completed with no evidence of disease for at least two years; demyelinating forms of Charcot‑Marie‑tooth disease; contraindication to any study drug component including prior anaphylactic reaction; ongoing systemic corticosteroid therapy; known active central nervous system lymphoma involvement; active infection necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy; known human immunodeficiency virus infection; known active hepatitis C virus infection; psychiatric or substance‑use disorders that would impede compliance with study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: < 22 years. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: De novo acute myeloid leukemia (per WHO 2016). Cancer burden allowed: Bone marrow involvement with ≥20 % blasts (or <20 % blasts with qualifying genetic lesions) ± extramedullary disease; excludes secondary AML, therapy‑related myeloid neoplasms, acute promyelocytic leukemia, mixed‐phenotype acute leukemia, Philadelphia chromosome‑positive AML, juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia, AML evolving from myelodysplastic syndromes. Prior treatment required: None mandated. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior anti‑cancer therapy except hydroxyurea, all‑trans retinoic acid, corticosteroids (any route), or diagnostic/intrathecal therapy administered at presentation. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Fanconi anaemia.
Shwachman‑Diamond syndrome.
Constitutional trisomy 21 or mosaic trisomy 21.
Telomere biology disorders.
Germline predisposition syndromes raising toxicity risk with AML therapy.
Concurrent second malignancy.
Persistent cardiac dysfunction defined as left ventricular ejection fraction < 50 % (or fractional shortening < 24 %) at baseline.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding at enrollment.
Inability or unwillingness to comply with required contraception measures for individuals of reproductive potential.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 2 to ≤21 years. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: De novo acute myeloid leukemia with FLT3 internal tandem duplication (ITD) having allelic ratio > 0.1 (as measured by molecular oncology). Cancer burden allowed: Same as Space 1. Prior treatment required: None mandated. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior anti‑cancer therapy except hydroxyurea, all‑trans retinoic acid, corticosteroids, or intrathecal therapy given at diagnosis. Biomarkers required: FLT3‑ITD mutation with allelic ratio > 0.1 confirmed before enrollment. Biomarkers excluded: FL​T3 wild‑type or FLT3‑mutated variants other than ITD meeting the allelic‑ratio threshold.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 year to ≤ 39 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: acute myeloid leukemia (World Health Organization classified), relapsed OR de‑novO OR secondary. Cancer burden allowed: receipt of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant AND achievement of measurable residual disease‑negative remission prior to first dose of study medication. Prior treatment required: planning to undergo allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (any donor type, any graft source, any conditioning regimen). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: FLT3 internal tandem duplication mutation.

Age range allowed: 1 year to ≤ 39 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: myelodysplastic syndrome. Histology allowed: myelodysplastic syndrome (World Health Organization classified), relapsed OR de‑novO OR secondary. Cancer burden allowed: receipt of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant AND attainment of measurable residual disease‑negative remission prior to first dose of study medication. Prior treatment required: planning to undergo allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (any donor type, any graft source, any conditioning regimen). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: FLT3 internal tandem duplication mutation.

Age range allowed: 1 year to ≤ 38 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: treatment‑related myeloid neoplasm (therapy‑associated myelodysplastic syndrome/myelogenous leukemia). Histology allowed: therapy‑related myelodysplastic syndrome or therapy‑related acute myeloid leukemia (World Health Organization classified), relapsed OR de‑novO OR secondary. Cancer burden allowed: receipt of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant AND achievement of measurable residual disease‑negative remission prior to first dose of study medication. Prior treatment required: planning to undergo allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (any donor type, any graft source, any conditioning regimen). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: gastrointestinal stromal tumor. Histology allowed: KIT‑mutated gastrointestinal stromal tumor (any activating KIT mutation except T670X). Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease with at least one measurable lesion. Prior treatment required: previous imatinib therapy with documented disease progression on imatinib. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: tumor harboring a KIT activating mutation. Biomarkers excluded: T670X KIT mutation or non‑KIT driving mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active central nervous system metastases. Uncontrolled intercurrent illness, including predefined cardiac disease. Corrected QT interval greater than 470 ms. Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50 %. Major surgery performed within two weeks before starting study treatment. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Significant gastrointestinal structural abnormalities impairing oral administration of study drugs. Ongoing clinically significant hemorrhage (excluding minor hemorrhoidal or gingival bleeds). Any concurrent malignant neoplasm judged likely to confound assessment of study outcomes.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (metastatic or unresectable). Histology allowed: Any solid‐tumor histology. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease or locally advanced unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: No minimum number of prior systemic therapies; for tumor entities in which a poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor is approved as standard of care, documentation of prior PARP inhibitor therapy is required, whereas for all other tumor types prior PARP inhibition may be absent or disease may be PARP‑inhibitor‑resistant. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic loss‑of‑function mutation (somatic or germline) in any of the following DNA‑damage‑repair genes—as determined by a CLIA‑certified assay: BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, RAD51C, RAD51D, ATM, BARD1, BLM, BRIP1, CDK12, FANCA, FANCC, FANCD2, FANCE, FANCF, FANCM, MRE11A, NBN, RAD50, RAD51B. Biomarkers excluded: UGT1A1 genotype showing homozygosity for *28 (TA)_7_TAA_, homozygosity for *6 (G71R), or compound heterozygosity (*28 plus *6).
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of significant untreated or unstable cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure; uncontrolled hypertension; clinically significant arrhythmias or prolonged baseline QTc (> 480 ms).
Active infections requiring intravenous antibiotics; unresolved bacterial, fungal, or mycobacterial infections.
Known active hepatitis B or C infection not adequately suppressed; uncontrolled HIV infection.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus or endocrine disorders affecting study assessments.
Significant pulmonary comorbidity such as ongoing pneumonia, interstitial lung disease, or prior radiation‑induced pneumonitis.
Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C) or unexplained elevation of liver enzymes greater than 1.5× upper limit of normal despite supportive care.
Renal insufficiency with estimated glomerular filtration rate < 60 ml/min/1.73 m².
Clinically evident symptomatic brain metastasis requiring corticosteroids or imminent local therapy (unless stable, off steroids ≥ 1 month as noted in inclusion).
Pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness to practice adequate contraception throughout study and for four months thereafter.
Concurrent participation in another therapeutic clinical trial or receipt of prohibited investigational agents outside allowable washout windows.
Allergic reaction to novobiacin or structurally related compounds.
Use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4/5 modulators, strong BCRP/ABC​G2, OATP1B1/B3, OATP2B1, OAT1/3 substrate medicines within disallowed intervals.
Concomitant intake of dietary/herbal products known to cause hepatotoxicity.
Medications posing a recognized risk of torsades de pointes or marked QTc prolongation (except mandated anti‑emetics such as granisetron).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable locoregional disease. Prior treatment required: Continuous EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor (erlotinib, gefitinib, afatinib, or osimertinib) for ≥6 weeks with documented radiographic progression while on therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Presence of an EGFR activating mutation (G719X, exon 19 deletion, L858R, or L861Q) **and** absence of EGFR T790M mutation when the preceding EGFR TKI was erlotinib, gefitinib, or afatinib. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of other active malignancy within 3 years (exceptions: adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, superficial bladder Ta/Tis/T1, curatively treated in‑situ disease).
Evidence of small‑cell or combined small‑cell/non‑small‑cell histology.
Active or untreated central nervous system metastasis or spinal cord compression; symptomatic brain metastases requiring steroids or anticonvulsants.
Recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA Class II‑IV), unstable angina, or significant cardiac arrhythmias.
Left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%.
Clinically significant ECG abnormalities or QTc prolongation (>470 ms women, >450 ms men) persisting over serial assessments.
Use of medications that markedly prolong QT interval or predispose to torsades de pointes.
Congenital long QT syndrome, familial sudden cardiac death before age 40, or other inherited channelopathies.
Interstitial lung disease, pulmonary fibrosis, severe radiation pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis/ILD at screening.
Severe uncontrolled hypertension (SBP > 180 mmHg or DBP > 110 mmHg).
Chronic systemic glucocorticoids exceeding 10 mg prednisone daily or other immunosuppressive therapy.
Significant ocular pathology such as corneal disease.
Unresolved toxicity from prior anticancer therapy greater than grade 1 (aside from alopecia).
Known active hepatitis B or C infection, or uncontrolled HIV infection.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive during the study period.

Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable locoregional disease. Prior treatment required: At least one prior platinum‑based chemotherapy regimen **and** prior EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy with documented radiographic progression while on EGFR TKI. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 - 30 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: neuroblastoma. Histology allowed: neuroblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory high‑risk disease (either documented recurrence after completion of frontline high‑risk therapy, progression during frontline high‑risk therapy, or primary resistance/non‑partial response after ≥4 cycles of intense induction) PLUS evidence of active disease (measurable tumor ≥10 mm on MRI/CT, or MIBG‑avid lesion, or FDG‑PET‑avid lesion) AND NOT isolated bone‑marrow‑only involvement; untreated central nervous system metastatic disease not permitted. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior receipt of naxitamab; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant; prior discontinuation of irinotecan or temozolomide because of unacceptable toxicity; prior discontinuation of GD2‑targeted immunotherapy because of unacceptable toxicity other than allergic reaction. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Chronic diarrhea lasting >2 weeks greater than grade 1; Pregnancy or current breastfeeding; Clinically significant arrhythmias causing symptoms or requiring medication; Severe intercurrent illnesses; Uncontrolled infections; Major organ dysfunction graded higher than CTCAE Grade 2 (excluding permissible hepatic, renal, hematologic parameters); Requirement to take immunosuppressive systemic steroids or immune-modulating agents; Concurrent anti‑cancer therapies or radiotherapy; Recent (<14 days) use of valproic acid, strong CYP3A4 inducers, strong CYP3A4 inhibitors, or strong UGT1A1 inhibitors.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 1 - 30 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: ganglioneuroblastoma. Histology allowed: ganglioneuroblastoma. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome/hepatic VOD of any severity.
Relapse confined solely to central nervous system, testes, or other exclusive extramedullary sites.
Previous treatment with inotuzumab ozogamicin.
Active optic nerve or retinal involvement.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional investigational study.
Use of other antitumor agents (excluding allowable hydroxyurea and intrathecal chemotherapy).
Therapies to prevent or treat graft‑vs‑host disease or organ rejection (cyclosporine, tacrolimus, etc.).
Systemic corticosteroids except as permitted for cytoreduction/pre‑mediation/pituitary support.
Known HIV infection, chronic hepatitis B or C infection.
Uncontrolled active infection (documented bacteremia, febrile illness with source, recent fungemia, active antiviral therapy, Clostridioides difficile pending, etc.).
Presence of inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes (Bloom, ataxia‑telangiectasia, Fanconi anemia, Kostmann, Shwachman‑Diamond, etc.).
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or inability/unwillingness to use adequate contraception according to gender‑specific recommendations.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 1 year (no upper limit). Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Primary immune deficiency‐related lymphoproliferative disorder. Histology allowed: Epstein‑Barr virus‑positive lymphoproliferative disease (biopsy‑proven or positive cerebrospinal fluid cytology). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease (radiographic progression per Lugano classification or failure to attain complete or partial response after standard first‑line therapy); disease may be confined to systemic sites, confined to central nervous system, or involve both. Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of systemic therapy **and** documentation of disease progression or inadequate response according to Lugano criteria. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior administration of non‑tabelecleucel Epstein‑Barr virus‑specific cytotoxic T‑cell products; prior allogeneic haematopoietic cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation. Biomarkers required: Tumour demonstrated Epstein‑Barr virus positivity by tissue staining or PCR (required for enrolment and will be verified at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active Burkitt lymphoma, T‑cell lymphoma, NK/T‑cell lymphoma, Hodgkin lymphoma, plasmablastic lymphoma, transformed lymphoma, or other malignant neoplasms requiring systemic therapy.
Active serious infections (e.g., uncontrolled adenoviral infection) or current infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Known Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
Grade ≥ 2 acute graft‑versus‑host disease or extensive chronic GVHD.
Requirement for vasoactive medication or mechanical ventilation at screening.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Inability/unwillingness to use effective contraception throughout study participation and until 90 days after last dose.
Ongoing corticosteroid therapy exceeding 0.5 mg/kg/day prednisolone-equivalent, continuous methotrexate, or extrcorporeal photopheresis (except protocol‑allowed peri‑enrollment dexamethasone for CNS disease).
Life expectancy less than 60 days or any concomitant severe, uncontrolled comorbidity posing unacceptable risk.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: ovarian cancer. Histology allowed: ovarian carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: advanced unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: documented disease progression on most recent standard therapy and refractory or intolerant to therapy normally benefiting this condition. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CCNE1 amplification (detected by next-generation sequencing on tissue or liquid sample, assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
* Presence of a locally advanced solid tumor amenable to potentially curative surgery, radiotherapy, or definitive chemoradiation.
* History of another malignant neoplasm (except adequately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, cervical carcinoma in situ, localized prostate cancer, or other pre-specified exceptions).
* History of lymphohistiocytic or lymphoid hyperplasia; hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.
* Incomplete recovery from toxicities of preceding anticancer therapy (baseline toxicity greater than grade 1).
* Clinically significant cardiovascular event occurring within six months prior to enrollment.
* Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.
* Current symptomatic/interstitial lung disease requiring therapeutic intervention.
* Significant ophthalmologic histories such as uveitis, retinopathies, severe corneal disease, or other active ocular conditions needing ongoing treatment.
* Recent postoperative wound healing issues (major surgery within one month or minor surgery within ten days before dosing).
* Confirmed active Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection, active Hepatitis B, or active Hepatitis C infection.
* Previous participation in an investigator-sponsored study involving a selective or nonspecific CDK2 inhibitor or degraders.
* Underlying hepatic impairment classified as Child‑Pugh B or C, or any other serious liver disease.
* Receipt of palliative radiation therapy within fourteen days or any form of radiation therapy within four weeks prior to day 1 of cycle 1.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of transformation to aggressive lymphoma. Symptomatic or untreated severe hyperviscosity syndrome (IgM >4000 mg/dL) when plasma exchange cannot be performed promptly. Concurrent participation in another investigational study. Residual toxicities from prior anticancer therapy exceeding Grade 1 (except alopecia and peripheral neuropathy). Uncontrolled active infection necessitating inpatient care or intravenous antibiotics within four weeks of enrollment. Significant cardiac impairment (NYHA class III–IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction <45%, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months, uncontrolled arrhythmias). Serious central nervous system pathology (uncontrolled seizures, recent stroke, psychosis, dementia, cerebral vasculitis, encephalitis). Requirement for supplemental oxygen at rest to keep saturation >92%. Recent systemic glucocorticoid (>20 mg prednisolone equivalent daily) or other potent immunosuppressants within 28 days for non‑study reasons. Documented central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia (CNS lesions, leptomeningeal disease) or ongoing CNS disease from another malignancy. Pregnancy, lactation, or inability/unwillingness to comply with mandated contraceptive measures throughout the study and for twelve months thereafter. Current alcohol or illicit drug misuse, unmanaged psychiatric illness, or socially unstable circumstances limiting protocol adherence. Known hypersensitivity to epcoritamab or structurally related substances. Administration of a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within four weeks preceding study start.

Prior treatment excluded: Any systemic chemotherapy or immunotherapy for diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma other than up to seven days of corticosteroids, up to one cycle of R‑CHOP, or prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy; anthracycline‑containing regimens; anti‑CD20 antibodies; other monoclonal antibodies; cytotoxic drugs administered within the preceding five years; anti‑CD20 antibodies within three months; any monoclonal antibody within three months; investigational therapy within twenty‑eight days; live vaccines within twenty‑eight days. Biomarkers required: CD20 positivity demonstrated on tumor tissue (assessed at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe allergic or anaphylactic reaction to humanized or murine monoclonal antibodies or to dexamethasone/systemic corticosteroids.
Prior solid-organ or stem‑cell transplantation.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Active infection or reactivated latent infection requiring intravenous antibiotics within two weeks of dosing.
Known HIV infection or HTLV‑1 seropositivity.
Clinically significant liver disease—including active viral hepatitis, cirrhosis, or ongoing excessive alcohol consumption.
Significant or uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (NYHA class III/IV, acute coronary syndrome within six months, unstable arrhythmias, or unstable angina).
Severe pulmonary disease compromising oxygen exchange.
Recent (<3 months) cerebrovascular accident, intracranian haemorrhage, or other substantial neurological event.
Grade 2 or worse peripheral neuropathy or demyelinating Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease.
Major surgical procedure within four weeks prior to registration (excluding diagnostic biopsies).
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy (stable hypothyroidism on levothyroxine or Type 1 diabetes on insulin are exempted).
Evidence or strong suspicion of hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of interstitial lung disease or active pulmonary fibrosis.
Active tuberculosis infection.
Uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus infection or detectable viral load.
Untreated or inadequately controlled hepatitis B or C infection.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease beyond NYHA functional classification IIb.
Severe hepatic impairment (total bilirubin >1.2 mg/dL or ALT/AST >3 × upper limit of normal).
Renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min).
Pregnancy or lactation.
Performance status poorer than ECOG 0‑1.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: male and female. Cancer type allowed: triple‑negative invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic (clinical stage IV) disease or unresectable locally advanced or recurrent disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: more than 2 prior lines of chemotherapy in the metastatic setting; more than 1 prior line of checkpoint inhibitor therapy in the metastatic setting; prior receipt of sacituzumab‑govitecan. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor‑negative (<5%) and progesterone receptor‑negative (<5%) and HER2‑negative (determined by IHC/FISH) and known tumor PD‑L1 status (to be assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease unless adequately treated and neurologically stable; active untreated infectious diseases including HIV/AIDS, hepatitis B or C; active autoimmune disease likely to worsen with immunomodulatory agents; past or current symptomatic interstitial lung disease; uncontrolled asthma; current symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA Class III-IV, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, recent stroke or TIA within six months; uncontrolled hypertension; significant cardiac arrhythmias; baseline left ventricular ejection fraction below institution’s lower limit of normal; concurrent antitumor therapy; prior solid organ or hematopoietic stem‑cell transplant; neuromuscular disorder with elevated creatine kinase; history of acute or chronic pancreatitis; retinal vascular occlusive disease or other severe ophthalmologic conditions; requirement for therapeutic vitamin K antagonist anticoagulation; lingering grade > 1 adverse events from earlier therapy (except alopecia or sensory neuropathy ≤Grade 2); severe hypersensitivity to monoclonal antibodies; live vaccinations within 28 days before dosing (inactive vaccines permissible); pregnant or lactating individuals; current alcohol or illicit drug dependence; incarceration; psychiatric, psychosocial, or other health circumstances deemed by investigators to pose unacceptable risks or impede adherence.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 12 months to 18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma. Histology allowed: biopsy‑proved diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma with documented histone mutation. Cancer burden allowed: locally confined disease without distant dissemination; measurable intracranial lesion. Prior treatment required: completion of definitive external beam radiation therapy finished 6 to 10 weeks (≈42–70 days) prior to enrollment. Prior treatment excluded: receipt of temozolomide during radiation; any anticancer systemic therapy (chemotherapy, targeted agents, immunotherapy) aside from radiation. Biomarkers required: histone mutation confirmed by pathological testing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Autoimmune or immune-mediated disorders; active respiratory disorder or infection; active viral infection; inability to meet performance status thresholds (Karnofsky ≤50 % or Lansky <50); body surface area less than 0.35 m²; inadequate organ function (renal, hepatic, cardiac insufficiency); uncontrolled concurrent illness; pregnancy or breastfeeding; known hypersensitivity to investigational product components.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active brain metastases or primary central nervous system tumors.
History of leptomeningeal metastases.
Active or chronic, uncontrolled bacterial, viral, or fungal infection(s) requiring systemic therapy.
Known human immunodeficiency virus positive status or active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease.
Corrected QT (QTcF) interval greater than 470 ms (greater than 450 ms for non‑pleural mesothelioma cohort).
Additional active malignancy that may confound the assessment of study endpoints.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Prior treatment with a TEAD inhibitor.

Uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia not attributable solely to lymphoma.
Active hepatitis C infection (even if monitored) or active hepatitis B infection (despite prophylaxis).
Previous malignant neoplasm unless cured with curative intent and disease free ≥ 3 years (exceptions: non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ of cervix or breast, Gleason 6 prostate cancer observed).
New York Heart Association class III–IV congestive heart failure.
Myocardial infarction within 6 months of screening.
Unstable angina within 3 months of screening.
Clinically significant untreated arrhythmia or ventricular tachyarrhythmia/sustained ventricular fibrillation/torsade de pointe within 6 months.
Mobitz II second‑degree or complete atrioventricular block without a permanent pacemaker.
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic > 170 mmHg and diastolic > 105 mmHg on two successive readings).
Baseline corrected QT interval > 480 milliseconds.
Inability to swallow capsules or marked gastrointestinal malabsorption syndromes.
Concomitant use of potent cytochrome P450 3A inducers.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during the study.
Severe or uncontrolled concurrent medical illnesses limiting compliance with study assessments.
Incapacity to follow protocol‑mandated restrictions or attend scheduled visits.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: early‐stage disease with pathological tumour size pT1c‑pT3, node‑negative (pN0) disease (isolated tumour cells counted as N0), no distant metastasis. Prior treatment required: completion of definitive breast surgery (total/mastectomy, skin‑sparring/nipple‑saving mastectomy, or lumpectomy) with margins free of invasive tumor/DCIS (“no ink on tumor”), axillary staging by sentinel node biopsy, targeted axillary dissection, or axillary lymph node dissection, and optional short‑course endocrine therapy (< 6 weeks) initiated before enrolment; prematurity confirmed (≤ 50 y with menses within 12 months, or > 50 with menses + FSH/Estradiol in premenopausal range, or equivalent biochemical proof). Prior treatment excluded: any chemotherapy, trastuzumab/pertinent HER2‑directed therapy, radiation therapy, or endocrine therapy lasting longer than six weeks for the index breast cancer; hormonally based contraceptives continued through baseline; prior bilateral/metachronous invasive breast cancer requiring systemic therapy. Biomarkers required: estrogen‑receptor positivity (≥ 1 % nuclear stain) **and** progesterone‑receptor positivity (≥ 1 %) **and** human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negativity (**by** ASCO/CAP guideline), **and** Oncotype DX recurrence score 21‑25 **or** recurrence score 16‑20 **plus** high clinical risk (low grade > 3 cm, intermediate grade > 2 cm, or high grade > 1 cm). Biomarkers excluded: Mammoprint “high‑risk” or MP2 classification; HER2 amplification; lack of ER/PR positivity.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Evidence of clinically detectable metastatic disease  
• Tumour staged pT4, including inflammatory breast cancer  
• History of prior ipsilateral or contralateral invasive breast cancer (except synchronous or past DCIS/LCIS)  
• Prior receipt of radiation therapy, chemotherapy, or biological therapy for the current breast cancer (endocrine therapy > 6 weeks prohibited)  
• Pregnant or breastfeeding at registration; inadequate birth control compliance  
• Significant hematologic abnormalities (ANC < 1200/mm³, platelets < 100 k, hemoglobin < 10 g/dL)  
• Hepatobiliary impairment (bilirubin > ULN or > 1.5×ULN with benign cause; ALT/AST > 3×ULN)  
• Severe renal insufficiency (glomerular filtration rate < 30 ml/min/1.73 m²)  
• Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease or NYHA Class III/IV heart failure; prior exposure to cardiotoxic agents deemed unsafe  
• Active infections: untreated HIV, unresolved hepatitis B (unless suppressed with undetectable DNA) or hepatitis C (untreated or viremic)  
• Poor functional status (performance status > 2/Karnofsky < 60 %)  
• Concurrent serious medical illness limiting life expectancy to < 10 years  
• Non‑epithelial breast tumours (sarcoma, lymphoma, etc.).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: mantle cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: mantle cell lymphoma (World Health Organization 2022 classification). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease after up to five prior lines of therapy with measurable disease (≥1 nodal lesion >1.5 cm long axis or ≥1 extranodal lesion >1 cm long axis). Prior treatment required: 1 to 5 prior lines of systemic therapy that included an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody–containing regimen. Prior treatment excluded: prior B‑cell lymphoma‑2 inhibitor therapy; prior covalent or non‑covalent Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy (any agent other than zanubrutinib). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known central nervous system involvement by lymphoma.
Autologous stem cell transplantation or chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy within three months before first dose of study drug.
Allogenic stem cell transplantation within six months before first dose of study drug.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease.
History of stroke or intracranial hemorrhage within six months before first dose of study drug.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non‑squamous non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑squamous (e.g., adenocarcinoma, bronchial alveolar subtype, not squamous). Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: no systemic anticancer therapy for advanced/metastatic disease; adjuvant or neoadjuvant therapy permissible if completed ≥6 months before diagnosis of advanced/metastatic disease and without disease progression within 6 months after completion. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic NSCLC; any prior topoisomerase I‑containing agent; any prior TROP2‑targeted therapy; any prior anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, or anti‑PD‑L2 antibody; any prior other immune‑checkpoint modulator (e.g., CTLA‑4, OX40, CD137) or other immune checkpoint inhibitor. Biomarkers required: programmed death ligand‑1 tumour proportion score <50% (assessed centrally by PD‑L1 IHC 22C3 PharmDX); lack of identifiable actionable genomic alterations (absence of driver mutations/rearrangements considered targetable). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis; prior live vaccination within 30 days of starting study treatment; spinal cord compression or clinically active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; unstable or symptomatic brain metastases (must be radiographically stable ≥2 weeks off steroids); prolonged QTc (>470 ms) regardless of sex; myocardial infarction ≤6 months before day 1; serious cardiac arrhythmia requiring treatment; uncontrolled angina ≤6 months before day 1; left ventricular ejection fraction <50% or New York Heart Association class II–IV congestive heart failure (unless recovered to class I and LVEF ≥50%); uncontrolled hypertension (SBP >180 mm Hg or DBP >110 mm Hg); severe pulmonary comorbidity—including recent pulmonary embolism (<3 months), severe asthma, severe COPD, restrictive lung disease, substantial pleural effusions, or autoimmune/connective‑tissue diseases affecting lungs; prior investigational COVID‑19 vaccine; any condition compromising safe conduct of protocol or interpretation of data.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑small cell lung carcinoma (any subtype). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with grade 2 or higher peripheral neuropathy; current or past autoimmune disease requiring systemic steroids or immunosuppression (excluding vitiligo, resolved childhood asthma/atopy, isolated hypothyroidism, stable chronic skin conditions, celiac disease controlled by diet, or remote inactive disease); concomitant systemic corticosteroid (>10 mg/day prednisolone equivalent) or other immunosuppressants within 14 days of dosing; prior malignant tumors (other than non‑melanoma skin cancer, in‑situ bladder, gastric, breast, colorectal or cervical dysplasias) unless in complete remission ≥2 years before enrolment; history of solid organ transplantation; pregnancy or lactation; known congenital long QT syndrome or resting QTcF ≥470 ms; uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmia, interstitial lung disease, severe GI disorder causing diarrhoea, or psychiatric/socioeconomic factors limiting adherence.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma (including inflammatory breast cancer).
