general_exclusion_criteria: Active infection or uncontrolled infectious disease; symptomatic congestive heart failure classified as New York Heart Association Class II or greater; unstable angina; clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias; significant pulmonary disease limiting breathing at rest or minimal exertion; active COVID‑19 infection; active human immunodeficiency virus infection with detectable viremia; active hepatitis B or C infection; clinically significant ascites or pleural effusion; known hypersensitivity to paclitaxel; peripheral neuropathy grade 2 or higher; corrected QT interval exceeding 480 ms or risk factors for torsades de pointes (family long QT syndrome, untreated electrolyte abnormalities); planned concomitant use of drugs that markedly prolong QT/QTc interval; use of strong cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inhibitors or inducer agents; current pregnancy or lactation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Any (subject must have no evidence of active malignancy). Histology allowed: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non‑compliance that investigators deem interferes with study participation  
Uncontrolled psychiatric illness  
Female participant who is pregnant or breastfeeding  
Use of contraindicated concomitant medications not detailed elsewhere (e.g., strong CYP interactions)

Pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness to employ effective contraception throughout the study duration.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Astrocytoma. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable disease not suitable for surgical resection, transplant or radiofrequency ablation; up to 5 liver lesions with combined maximum dimension <20 cm and each lesion ≤15 cm; permits small‑volume extrahepatic disease whose summed longest dimensions <2 cm; Barcelona Clinic Liver Cancer stage Inter­mediate (B) or Advanced (C); measurable disease per RECIST; Child‑Pugh class A liver function. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic anticancer therapy; prior liver‑directed external beam radiation that would deliver excessive dose to normal liver tissue; prior yttrium‑90 radioembolisation (selective internal radiotherapy). Biomarkers required: documented hepatitis B virus status (chronic infection → undetectable HBV DNA on antiviral therapy; active infection → HBV DNA < 500 IU/mL) AND documented hepatitis C virus status; biomarkers assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of pneumonitis; uncontrolled congestive heart failure or NYHA class III-IV; significant coronary artery disease, recent myocardial infarction or stroke; uncontrolled hypertension; moderate-to-severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min); marked hepatic dysfunction (Child‑Pugh B or C); uncontrolled central nervous system metastases; active HIV infection; active tuberculosis; current systemic bacterial, fungal or viral infection requiring antimicrobial therapy; pregnant or lactating women; inability to tolerate study medications; concurrent participation in another interventional clinical trial.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=12 months and <30 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: neuroblastoma. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: high‑risk neuroblastoma per Children’s Oncology Group risk classification with relapsed or refractory disease and evaluable/measurable disease per International Neuroblastoma Response Criteria. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy or other gene‑modified immune‑effector cell therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or lactation. Active uncontrolled infection. Concurrent receipt of other investigational agents. Presence of an additional malignant neoplasm other than non‑melanomatous skin cancer or carcinoma in situ unless inactive/stable for ≥3 years. Uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis. Central nervous system disorders compromising assessment of neurotoxicity (e.g., stroke, hemorrhage, dementia, cerebellar disease, autoimmune CNS disease). Severe hypersensitivity reactions to study components. Positive serology for HIV, HBV, or HCV. Ongoing systemic corticosteroid therapy exceeding physiological replacement doses (inhaled steroids and pre‑medications permitted). Primary immunodeficiency or systemic autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppressive therapy within the past 2 years. Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illnesses. Inability to comply with study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Immunoglobulin M monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance. Histology allowed: No bone marrow infiltration. Cancer burden allowed: Predominantly sensory demyelinating neuropathy with Modified Rankin Scale ≥1 (progressive) or ≥2, and ECOG performance status 0‑2. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systemic chemotherapy, Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitors, other Waldenström macroglobulinaemia‑directed therapies (other than corticosteroids, intravenous immune globulin, anti‑CD20 antibodies). Biomarkers required: detectible IgM monoclonal paraprotein on serum immunofixation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of pneumonitis or other acute respiratory toxicities; uncontrolled heart failure (NYHA Class III‑IV) or recent myocardial infarction or unstable angina; recent cerebrovascular accident or intracranial haemorrhage within six months; uncontrolled arterial hypertension; significant arrhythmias; active central nervous system lymphoma; active bleeding or coagulopathy (incl. inherited clotting disorders); requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation with vitamin K antagonists; chronic liver disease reaching Child‑Pugh class C; estimated glomerular filtration rate < 30 ml/min; elevated total bilirubin >1.5×ULN, AST/ALT >2.5×ULN; prolonged PT/INR >2×ULN or aPTT >2×ULN (unless due to antiphospholipid antibodies); known human immunodeficiency virus infection; active hepatitis B (positive HBsAg) or untreated hepatitis C infection; any uncontrolled infectious process; recent vaccination with live attenuated vaccines (<4 weeks); concurrent enrollment in another therapeutic trial; inability to swallow oral medication; malabsorptive syndromes prohibiting enteral dosing; regular use of strong/moderate CYP3A inhibitors or inducers within seven days; continual use of proton‑pump inhibitors (switch to H₂ antagonist or antacid allowable); pregnancy, lactation, or plans to conceive during study period; inadequate contraception adherence for females of child‑bearing potential or males partnering with such women; refusal or inability to give informed consent.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinaemia. Histology allowed: Small lymphocyte proliferates with plasmacytoid differentiation, immunophenotype IgM⁺, CD5±, CD10⁻, CD19⁺, CD20⁺, CD22⁺, CD23⁻, CD25⁺, CD27⁺, FMC7⁺, CD103⁻, CD138⁻. Cancer burden allowed: Bone‑marrow infiltration characteristic of Waldenström macroglobulinaemia combined with predominantly sensory demyelinating neuropathy, Modified Rankin Scale ≥1 (progressive) or ≥2, and ECOG performance status 0‑2. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systematic chemotherapy, Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitors, other Waldenström macroglobulinaemia‑targeted therapies (other than corticosteroids, intravenous immune globulin, anti‑CD20 antibodies). Biomarkers required: detectible IgM monoclonal paraprotein on serum immunofixation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of pneumonitis or other acute respiratory toxicities; uncontrolled heart failure (NYHA Class III‑IV) or recent myocardial infarction or unstable angina; recent cerebrovascular accident or intracranial haemorrhage within six months; uncontrolled arterial hypertension; significant arrhythmias; active central nervous system lymphoma; active bleeding or coagulopathy (incl. inherited clotting disorders); requirement for therapeutic anticoagulation with vitamin K antagonists; chronic liver disease reaching Child‑Pugh class C; estimated glomerular filtration rate < 30 ml/min; elevated total bilirubin >1.5×ULN, AST/ALT >2.5×ULN; prolonged PT/INR >2×ULN or aPTT >2×ULN (unless due to antiphospholipid antibodies); known human immunodeficiency virus infection; active hepatitis B (positive HBsAg) or untreated hepatitis C infection; any uncontrolled infectious process; recent vaccination with live attenuated vaccines (<4 weeks); concurrent enrollment in another therapeutic trial; inability to swallow oral medication; malabsorptive syndromes prohibiting enteral dosing; regular use of strong/moderate CYP3A inhibitors or inducers within seven days; continual use of proton‑pump inhibitors (switch to H₂ antagonist or antacid allowable); pregnancy, lactation, or plans to conceive during study period; inadequate contraception adherence for females of child‑bearing potential or males partnering with such women; refusal or inability to give informed consent.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: <50 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Osteosarcoma. Histology allowed: Osteosarcoma (any subtype). Cancer burden allowed: Up to four pulmonary nodules per lung, each ≤3 cm, at least one nodule ≥3 mm, all lesions judged resectable, no extrapulmonary metastatic disease, no centrally located lesions necessitating lobectomy or pneumonectomy, no chest‑wall or mediastinal metastatic deposits, no clinically significant pleural effusion. Prior treatment required: Systemic chemotherapy comprising cisplatin together with doxorubicin **or** an ifosfamide‑based regimen administered for newly diagnosed osteosarcoma. Prior treatment excluded: Prior therapeutic pulmonary surgery for lung metastases; unresectable primary tumor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable or unwilling to provide written informed consent.
Non‑completion of applicable Institutional Review Board, U.S. Food and Drug Administration, or National Cancer Institute administrative requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: <50 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Osteosarcoma. Histology allowed: Osteosarcoma (any subtype). Cancer burden allowed: Up to four pulmonary nodules per lung, each ≤3 cm, at least one nodule ≥3 mm, all lesions judged resectable, no extrapulmonary metastatic disease, no centrally located lesions necessitating lobectomy or pneumonectomy, no chest‑wall or mediastinal metastatic deposits, no clinically significant pleural effusion. Prior treatment required: Completion of systemic chemotherapy comprising cisplatin together with doxorubicin **or** an ifosfamide‑based regimen that constituted standard adjuvant/neoadjuvant therapy for the original primary osteosarcoma. Prior treatment excluded: Prior therapeutic pulmonary surgery for lung metastases; unresectable primary tumor. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable or unwilling to provide written informed consent.
Non‑completion of applicable Institutional Review Board, U.S. Food and Drug Administration, or National Cancer Institute administrative requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: follicular lymphoma. Histology allowed: grade 1‑3A follicular lymphoma AND CD20 positive AND no histologic transformation. Cancer burden allowed: meets at least one modified GELF criterion (symptomatic adenopathy OR organ function impairment due to disease OR constitutional B symptoms OR nodal or extranodal tumor mass >7 cm OR >3 nodal sites >3 cm OR local compressive symptoms OR splenomegaly >16 cm craniocaudal dimension OR clinically significant pleural or peritoneal effusion OR leukemic phase >5×10⁹/L circulating malignant cells OR rapidly progressive disease OR renal infiltration OR bone lesions) AND not stage I disease. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic therapy for follicular lymphoma (radiation therapy or short‑course steroids allowable but not required). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic anticancer therapy for follicular lymphoma. Biomarkers required: Tumor CD20 positivity. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients requiring chronic systemic immunosuppressive therapy (prednisone >10 mg/day) ; patients with bulky cervical adenopathy threatening airway patency ; candidates for definitive curative radiation therapy ; recent major surgery or trauma within 4 weeks before study start ; active hepatitis B virus DNA‐positive infection or active hepatitis C virus RNA‑positive infection (HBV carriers allowed with prophylaxis, cured HCV allowed) ; known HIV infection ; active bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic or other infection at enrollment (aside from superficial nail fungus) ; prior malignancy other than non‑melanoma skin cancer or in‑situ cervical or breast carcinoma unless disease‑free ≥2 years ; receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 1 week of entry or thereafter ; uncontrolled concurrent medical illnesses limiting compliance ; cardiovascular events within past 6 months (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, congestive heart failure NYHA class III/IV, left ventricular ejection fraction <45%) or significant arrhythmias/QTc prolongation >470 msec ; pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during study ; prior solid organ or allogenic stem cell transplantation ; history of haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis ; active autoimmune disorders (unless remotely resolved and approved) ; severe hypersensitivity to anti‑CD20 antibodies or components of epcoritamab ; active central nervous system lymphoma ; peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 2 ; chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy ≤100 days before dosing ; investigational agent administration ≤4 weeks (or five half‑lives) before dosing ; conventional chemotherapeutic or non‑investigational antineoplastics (other than CD20 monoclonals) ≤4 weeks (or five half‑lifes) before dosing ; active SARS‑CoV‑2 infection or inadequate recovery/testing parameters .

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Residual invasive disease in the breast and/or lymph nodes after neoadjuvant chemotherapy (if no residual invasive disease in breast, lymph node must contain ≥2 mm invasive disease); no clinical or radiographic evidence of distant metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Completed neoadjuvant chemotherapy; definitive breast surgery with pathologically clear margins (lumpectomy with subsequent whole‑breast irradiation or mastectomy) together with axillary surgery; completion of any planned postoperative radiation; detection of circulating tumour DNA (any tumor‑specific mutation) in a blood sample obtained after finishing all locoregional therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior administration of sacituzumab govitecan, irinotecan, or any topoisomerase I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugate for early‑stage disease; receipt of adjuvant chemotherapy (post‑operative chemotherapy). Biomarkers required: Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (by ISH ratio < 2.0 or average HER2 copy number < 4 signals/cell, **or** IHC score 0 or 1+); estrogen receptor negative (**< 10 %**) AND progesterone receptor negative (**< 10 %**) in the primary tumor; presence of circulating tumour DNA detected by centralized assay. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on imaging; symptomatic interstitial lung disease; unresolved treatment‑related toxicities greater than Grade 1 (excluding alopecia and peripheral neuropathy); major surgical procedures (other than diagnostic biopsies) within 4 weeks preceding study treatment or anticipated during the study; systemic infectious illnesses requiring therapy (active bacterial, viral, fungal infections, active tuberculosis); uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including NYHA class III‑IV heart failure, myocardial infarction within 6 months, unstable angina, or serious arrhythmias; serious liver disease such as cirrhosis; active bleeding diatheses; uncontrolled type I or type II diabetes mellitus; grade ≥ 2 unmanaged hyperlipidaemia, hypertriglyceridaemia, or hypercalcaemia; active autoimmune disease needing systemic immunosuppression (exceptions noted for controlled hypothyroidism, celiac disease, mild skin conditions); impaired gastrointestinal absorption due to active inflammatory disease, obstruction, unresected small bowel, or severe diarrhoea/vomiting/nausea; congenital long QT syndrome or screened QTc > 480 ms; documented HIV infection, hepatitis B surface antigen positivity, or hepatitis C RNA positivity; concurrent use of strong UGT1A1 inhibitors or inducers; receipt of a live attenuated vaccine within 4 weeks before starting study treatment; systemic glucocorticoid or other immunosuppressive therapy within 2 weeks of enrolment (brief pulses allowable); pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability to comply with effective contraception requirements; inadequate organ function outside predefined lab limits (hematologic, hepatic, renal parameters); Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status ≥ 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: any invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable locally recurrent or metastatic disease (must be evaluable/measurable per RECIST v1.1). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior chemotherapy or HER2‑targeted therapy for advanced disease; prior trastuzumab deruxtecan administered in the adjuvant or neoadjuvant setting. Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive tumor (confirmed by centralized laboratory or validated local assay) and documented estrogen receptor and progesterone receptor status. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Current participation in another therapeutic clinical trial (aside from translational studies).
Known active uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease (patients with historically treated, stable CNS lesions may be eligible).
Concurrent malignant disease or malignancy diagnosed within the preceding five years, except adequately treated cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, basal/squamous cell skin carcinomas, or Stage I uterine cancer.
Allergy or hypersensitivity to investigational product components.
Recent extensive radiotherapy (wide-field or >30 % bone marrow irradiation) shortly before study start.
Major surgical procedure or significant trauma immediately before study commencement.
Active cardiovascular disease—including recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, New York Heart Association class II–IV congestive heart failure, poorly controlled hypertension, symptomatic pericarditis, left ventricular ejection fraction < 55 %, clinically significant arrhythmias, or prolonged corrected QT interval (>470 ms in females, >450 ms in males)—that poses unacceptable risk.
Severe pulmonary impairment due to ongoing respiratory illness, substantial COPD, restrictive lung disease, recent pulmonary embolism, interstitial lung disease/history requiring steroid therapy, or prior pneumonectomy.
Pregnancy, lactation, or refusal/inadequacy of highly effective contraception measures.
Current active hepatitis B or C infection, unresolved hepatitis B carrier state, or detectable hepatitis C RNA.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection or other primary immune deficiency.
Any other active uncontrolled infectious process.
Receipt of live or attenuated vaccination within four weeks prior to dosing.
Uncontrolled seizures, destabilizing neurologic disorders, or serious unmanaged psychiatric conditions compromising adherence or safety.
Other concurrent severe or uncontrolled medical conditions deemed unsuitable by investigators.
Substance dependence or misuse posing safety/compliance concerns.
Incapacity or unwillingness to abide by study assessments and follow‑up schedule.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor (any origin except pancreas). Histology allowed: carcinomas of epithelial origin. Cancer burden allowed: locoregional unresectable or distant metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: receipt of prior standard therapy appropriate for tumor type and stage. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: specific KRAS G12 mutation or any RAS mutation detected by DNA sequencing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Primary central nervous system tumors
Active, untreated brain metastases
Known or suspected impaired gastrointestinal function prohibiting swallowing or absorbing oral medication
Unstable or clinically significant concomitant medical conditions compromising safety, survival expectancy, or compliance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=16 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: melanoma. Histology allowed: malignant melanoma. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: BRAF V600 mutation or non‑V600 class II or class III BRAF alteration identified in tumor tissue or circulating tumor DNA. Biomarkers excluded: NA

History of retinal vein occlusion or existing neuromuscular disorder associated with elevated creatine kinase for participants planned to receive binimetat ib.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of central nervous system metastases or spinal cord compression with progressive neurologic signs or needing steroids; known intracranial haemorrhage or bleeding disorders; visceral crisis, lymphangitic spread, or leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; currently active interstitial lung disease or recent radiation pneumonitis; resting QTcF greater than 470 ms, congenital long QT syndrome, or family history thereof; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including hypertension; past malignancies except appropriately treated basal/squamous skin cancer, cured cervical intra‑epithelial neoplasia, prostate cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or other solid tumours disease‑free ≥ 3 years; known HIV infection, active viral/bacterial/fungal infections (hepatitis B, hepatitis C, TB, COVID‑19); concomitant prohibited medicines/herbal products that cannot be stopped two weeks beforehand; anticipated major surgery within four weeks of starting study drug; inability to abide by protocol assessments; prior high‑dose chemotherapy with stem‑cell rescue; hypersensitivity to INX‑315, fulvestrant, or abemaciclib components; conditions impeding safe oral intake or causing significant gastrointestinal malabsorption; serious untreated concurrent medical illnesses judged likely to interfere with study conduct.

Cancer burden allowed: Candidate for reduced‑intensity conditioned HLA‑matched related or haploidentical allogeneic stem cell transplant; high risk for post‑transplant relapse; measurable residual disease not required. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: FLT3 inhibitor maintenance therapy or IDH inhibitor maintenance therapy or BCR‑ABL inhibitor maintenance therapy; sirolimus containing graft‑versus‑host disease prophylaxis. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Adult candidates predicted to derive greater benefit from myeloablative conditioning; extramedullary leukemia in central nervous system or testes; prior allogeneic stem cell transplant occurring within seven days of intended CIML NK infusion; prior solid organ transplantation; known serious allergic reactions to cellular products or components resembling interleukin‑2; uncontrolled concomitant illnesses such as active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia, or major psychiatric disorder limiting adherence; pregnancy or lactation; HIV seropositivity; active, unmanaged hepatitis B or C infection; prior invasive malignancies unless in sustained remission ≥2 years or qualifying low‑risk skin/prostate lesions as detailed; history of grade 2 or higher hemolytic anemia; receipt of systemic corticosteroids exceeding 10 mg prednisolone equivalents on the day of NK cell infusion; unresolved donor‑specific antibody issues with mean fluorescent intensity >1000 absent proper desensitisation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
1. Prior treatment with an oncolytic therapy.
2. Presence of visceral metastases.
3. Active herpetic infections or prior complications of HSV‑1 infection (e.g., herpetic keratitis or encephalitis).
4. History of organ graft rejection within 12 months.
5. Systemic infection requiring intravenous antibiotics or antivirals, or other serious infection within 60 days prior to dosing.
6. Intermittent or chronic use of systemic anti‑virals with anti‑herpetic activity (except when required for organ allograft preservation).
7. Requirement for CTLA‑4‑Ig medications.
8. Ongoing or recent (within 5 years) significant autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive treatment beyond routine transplant maintenance (vitiligo, resolved childhood asthma, type 1 diabetes, controlled hypothyroidism, or mild psoriasis not requiring systemic therapy are exempt).
9. Active infection with hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.
10. Transplant‑related viral infections (BK virus, Epstein-Barr virus, cytomegalovirus) within 3 months of enrollment; subjects with historic hepatitis B/C must have undetectable viral loads within 3 months.
11. Conditions requiring escalation of customary immunosuppressive regimen within 60 days of study drug administration.
12. Known active central nervous system metastases or carcinomatous meningitis.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Moderate or severe corneal epithelial disease (only mild change permissible).  
* Requirement to wear contact lenses during the study.  
* Participation in another interventional clinical investigation concurrently.  
* Receipt of high‑dose systemic corticosteroids (greater than prednisolone/prednisone 10 mg/day) contemporaneously with study treatment.  
* Pregnancy, lactation, or plans to become pregnant during the study period.  
* Serious cardiovascular illnesses including New York Heart Association class III–IV congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant untreated arrhythmias (second‑degree AV block or higher), myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident within the prior six months, or uncontrolled hypertension.  
* Advanced chronic obstructive pulmonary disease resulting in more than two inpatient admissions over the past twelve months.  
* Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus characterized by more than two episodes of diabetic ketoacidosis in the preceding twelve months.  
* Unstable liver or biliary disease.  
* Active acute bacterial, viral, or fungal infection.  
* Grade 2 or greater peripheral sensory/motor neuropathy persisting despite optimal supportive measures.  
* Known hypersensitivity to isatuximab, belantamab mafodotin, pomalidomide, or dexamethasone (excluding manageable rash to IMiDs).  
* Major surgical procedures performed within four weeks prior to day 1 cycle 1.  
* Administration of live or live‑attenuated vaccinations within thirty days prior to day 1 cycle 1.

Prior treatment required: Chemochemistry and/or radiation therapy within the preceding 60 months. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Total testosterone <348 ng/dL and/or free testosterone <70 pg/mL (mass‑spectrometry). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Hematocrit greater than 48%.
Serum creatinine greater than 2.5 mg/dL.
Prostatic specific antigen greater than 4 ng/mL or palpable nodules/indurations on digital rectal examination.
Severe untreated obstructive sleep apnoea.
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure.
Myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndrome, cardiac revascularisation procedure, cerebrovascular accident or arterial/thrombotic event occurring within six months before enrollment.
Known inherited thrombophilia (e.g., factor V Leiden).
Previous stroke leaving persistent neurological deficit.
Cognitive impairment preventing consent (Mini–Mental State Examination score < 24).
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (hemoglobin A₁c exceeding 10%).  
Body-mass index over 40 kg/m².  
History of bipolar disorder or schizophrenia.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of serious allergic reaction to components of study drugs; current active infection requiring systemic therapy; concurrent anticoagulation with warfarin; prior serotonin syndrome; uncontrolled comorbidities such as active tuberculosis, pneumonitis, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia; ongoing systemic immune suppression for autoimmune or inflammatory disorders (except permitted mild conditions); clinically significant electrocardiographic abnormalities; gastrointestinal disorder impairing oral drug absorption; receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 30 days preceding enrollment; pregnancy or lactation; active chronic viral hepatitis B or C infection.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Recent systemic anticancer therapy, biologic therapy, investigational agents, radiotherapy, or major surgery within prohibited intervals (excluded as washout periods).
• Chemotherapy, oral targeted therapy, or TKIs administered within five half‑lives before study entry (washout period – excluded).
• Participation in another investigational study concurrently.
• Diagnosis of hematologic lymphoma.
• Symptomatic central nervous system metastases that are neurologically unstable or require immediate radiation therapy.
• Untreated or progressive brain metastases deemed unsafe by investigators (asymptomatic/stable lesions permitted).
• Known hypersensitivity to abemaciclib or chemically related substances.
• Uncontrolled intercurrent medical illnesses—including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmia, or severe psychiatric disorders limiting compliance.
• Current pregnancy or breastfeeding; requires negative pregnancy test and appropriate contraception.
• Positive HIV serostatus.
• Active chronic hepatitis B virus or hepatitis C virus infection.
• Requirement for concomitant enzyme‑inducing anti‑epileptic drugs that cannot be replaced with non‑enzyme‑inducing alternatives.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Triple‑Negative Breast Cancer (estrogen receptor <10% and progesterone receptor <10% and HER2‑negative by ASCO/CAP testing). Cancer burden allowed: Clinically staged T1c–T4, N0–N2, M0 disease with pathologic complete response (no residual invasive disease in breast or lymph nodes) after neoadjuvant chemotherapy plus pembrolizumab. Prior treatment required: At least six cycles of neoadjuvant chemotherapy delivered concomitantly with pembrolizumab, completed before surgery; definitive breast surgery with negative margins (mastectomy or breast‑conserving surgery meeting margin rules); optional postoperative radiation permitted. Prior treatment excluded: Concurrent/investigational anti‑cancer agents at the time of registration. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor <10% and progesterone receptor <10% and HER2‑negative. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Presence of stage IV (distant metastatic) breast cancer  
• Any prior invasive breast cancer (contra­lateral or ipsilateral) – prior DCIS allowed  
• Evidence of recurrent disease after neoadjuvant therapy and surgery  
• Active liver disease (hepatitis B, hepatitis C, autoimmune hepatobiliary disorder, sclerosing cholangitis)  
• History of grade 3‑4 infusion reactions or hypersensitivity to pembrolizumab or mouse proteins  
• Requirement for chronic systemic glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisolone equivalents/day) or other immunosuppressants within the past two years (physiological hormone replacements exempt)  
• Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (NYHA functional classification poorer than class IIIB)  
• Poor performance status (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score greater than 2)  
• Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; women of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test ≤7 days before randomisation  
• Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection not controlled on antiretroviral therapy with detectable viral load within 6 months prior to enrolment.

general_exclusion_criteria: Uncontrolled hepatitis B infection despite antiviral testing protocol; Chronic liver disease classified as Child–Pugh B or C; Pregnant, nursing, or intending to conceive during study and up to stipulated post‑treatment interval; Severe cardiovascular disease—including unstable angina, recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, left ventricle ejection fraction ≤ 45%, NYHA class III/IV, symptomatic arrhythmias, or QTc > 480 msec; Active non‑central nervous system infection or ongoing antibiotic-requiring infection; Active major hemorrhage necessitating transfusion or intervention; Requirement for continuous anticoagulation does not preclude enrolment but underlying coagulopathy leading to bleeding risk excludes; Inability to ingest oral capsules; Recent (within 14 days or five half‑lives) receipt of moderate/strong CYP3A inhibitors or inducers; Participation in another interventional oncology trial concurrently; History of another malignancy except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, superficial bladder cancer, localized prostate cancer, or other solid tumors in long-term remission.

Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Inability to undergo brain MRI; history of (non‑infectious) pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy or steroids >10 mg prednisone-equivalent; requirement for high‑dose systemic corticosteroids (dexamethasone >4 mg/day or equivalent for >3 consecutive days within 2 weeks of registration); arterial thromboembolic event such as cerebral vascular accident within 3 months; clinically significant cardiovascular disease (uncontrolled/symptomatic arrhythmia, NYHA class III–IV heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within 6 months); uncontrolled hypertension despite optimal therapy; poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (HbA1c >8%) or steroid‑induced diabetes meeting same threshold; non‑healing wound, ulcer or bone fracture; known bleeding diathesis (von Willebrand disease, hemophilia); inability to swallow capsules or conditions markedly impairing GI absorption; concurrent potent CYP3A4/5 inducer or inhibitor usage not stopped appropriately; use of enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants without appropriate substitution; known HIV/AIDS infection; active hepatitis B (surface antigen positive) or active hepatitis C (detectable viral RNA); active tuberculosis infection; prior allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation; existing psychiatric or substance‑use disorder compromising compliance; pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child during study period up to 120 days after last dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 28 days to <18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Any disease (benign or malignant) treated with allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Diagnosis of hematopoietic stem cell transplant‑associated thrombotic microangiopathy confirmed by platelet count <50,000/µL or ≥50% reduction from peak post‑transplant value PLUS laboratory evidence of microangiopathic hemolysis (schistocytes and/or serum LDH greater than upper limit of normal and/or haptoglobin below lower limit of normal) AND at least one high‑risk feature (spot urine protein/creatinine ratio >2 mg/mg, serum creatinine >1.5× baseline, biopsy‑proven GI TMA, neurologic manifestation attributable to TMA, unexplained pericardial/pleural effusion, pulmonary hypertension, Grade III/IV graft‑versus‑host disease, or serum C5b‑9 >244 ng/mL). Prior treatment required: Receipt of an allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Prior treatment excluded: Current or recent (≤3 months) use of eculizumab, ravulizumab, or defibrotide for any indication (except documented therapeutic failure); receipt of any investigational drug or device within 4 weeks before enrollment. general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients who have not fully recovered from prior therapy–related adverse events higher than Grade 1 (aside from alopecia and fatigue).
Presence of active autoimmune disease that required systemic treatment (disease‑modifying agents, corticosteroids, or immunosuppressive drugs) within the preceding 2 years.
History of active tuberculosis.
Confirmed active hepatitis B virus infection or active hepatitis C virus infection.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection or AIDS.
Evidence of active, non‑infectious pneumonitis.
Any ongoing infection necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Another concurrently existing malignancy that is currently progressive or requires active treatment (exceptions: adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin carcinomas, in‑situ cervical cancer).
Pregnant, nursing, or planning conception/fatherhood during the study period up to 120 days after the last dose.
Contraindications to magnetic resonance imaging of the brain (e.g., implanted devices incompatible with MRI, severe allergic reaction to gadolinium contrast).
Receipt of a live viral vaccine within 30 days before initiating study drug (inactive seasonal influenza vaccine permissible).

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of grade ≥2 pneumonitis unrelated to current indication.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure NYHA class III/IV.
Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min).

Active central nervous system involvement or untreated/uncontrolled brain metastases.
Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; unwillingness to use effective contraceptive methods.
Inability to comply with study procedures or give informed consent due to psychiatric or cognitive disorders.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically or radiographically progressive/uncontrolled brain metastases or lepto­meningeal disease.  
Myocardial impairment causing New York Heart Association class II‑IV heart failure.  
Severe acute or chronic cardiac, pulmonary, hepatic, or renal insufficiency deemed unsafe for study participation.  
Uncontrolled intercurrent infection or ongoing antimicrobial therapy; infectious diseases not resolved at baseline.  
Active human immunodeficiency virus infection or receipt of antiretroviral therapy.  
Active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.  
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of effective contraception in females of childbearing potential.  
Other invasive malignancy diagnosed within the preceding 2 years, except adequately treated carcinoma in situ of the cervix, non‑melanoma skin cancer, stage I uterine cancer, or other cancers with anticipated cure.  
Recent major surgery, radiation therapy, or autologous/allogenic stem‑cell transplantation within designated washout periods (excluded here as procedural timing).  
Use of concomitant investigational agents outside allowable windows.  
Known hypersensitivity to components of the study drug formulation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Prior treatment excluded: any prior CLL‑directed therapy (including chemotherapy, immunotherapy, targeted therapy, radiation), prior anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody therapy, systemic corticosteroids exceeding 20 mg prednisone-equivalent daily at enrollment. Biomarkers excluded: active hepatitis B infection (hepatitis B surface antigen positive with detectable HBV DNA) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA).

general_exclusion_criteria: Participants unable to undergo magnetic resonance imaging; isolated ocular primary CNS lymphoma without brain or spinal cord involvement; documented serious hypersensitivity reaction (Grade ≥ 3) to pembrolizumab, high‑dose methotrexate, temozolomide, rituximab or their excipients; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within the past 2 years (excluding physiological replacements); receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days preceding first dose; concomitant malignant neoplasm that is progressing or requires active treatment within the past 3 years (except adequately treated superficial skin carcinomas or carcinoma in situ); history of non‑infectious pneumonitis needing steroids or existing pneumonitis; inadequately controlled diabetes mellitus (HbA₁c > 8%) including steroid‑induced cases; conditions impairing oral capsule ingestion or significant gastrointestinal malfunction (e.g., malabsorption, gastrectomy, extensive bowel resection, complete obstruction); concurrent use of moderate/strong CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers that cannot be stopped appropriately; usage of enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants not replaced 2 weeks before initiation; active hepatitis B surface antigen positivity or detectable hepatitis C viral RNA; active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection; underlying immunodeficiency or chronic systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents; prior allogeneic stem cell transplantation; clinically significant pleural effusions, ascites, or generalized edema; untreated major psychiatric illness or substance misuse compromising protocol compliance; pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child from screening until 120 days after the last administered dose.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of immune‑mediated pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Active symptomatic cardiovascular disease including recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, New York Heart Association class III/IV heart failure, or clinically significant arrhythmia.
Significant renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence.
Severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >3× ULN or AST/ALT >5× ULN).
Untreated or progressing central nervous system metastasis requiring steroids.
Known active infections with human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C.
Concurrent serious medical illness that would compromise participant safety or compliance.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Any concurrent second active malignancy requiring therapy (excluding early‑stage non‑melanoma skin cancers).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: All genders. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: squamous. general_exclusion_criteria:
- Enrollment prohibited for individuals with histological confirmation of small cell lung carcinoma.
- Participation disallowed if concurrently enrolled in another therapeutic clinical study (except non‑interventional studies or ongoing safety follow‑up).
- Individuals with symptomatic central nervous system metastases, central nervous system lesions equal to or larger than 1.5 cm, receipt of cranial irradiation within seven days preceding randomisation, expected need for cranial irradiation during the initial treatment cycle, or leptomeningeal carcinomatosis are excluded.
- Candidates with any other malignant neoplasm diagnosed within three years prior to enrolment, unless definitive curative therapy completed and no evidence of disease persists.
- Persons with active autoimmune disorders or chronic pulmonary diseases necessitating systemic therapy are excluded.
- Subjects exhibiting baseline peripheral neuropathy graded ≥ 2 (CTCAE v5.0) are excluded.
- Recent severe infectious episodes occurring within four weeks before randomisation lead to exclusion.
- Participants undergoing major surgical operations or experiencing serious traumatic injury within four weeks prior to randomisation are excluded.
- History of non‑infectious pneumonia that demanded systemic glucocorticoids, or existing interstitial lung disease, constitute exclusion criteria.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Stage I, Stage II, or Stage III (localised disease without distant metastasis; excludes inflammatory breast cancer [T4d]). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any endocrine therapy, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or investigational therapy for the current breast cancer; any systemic therapy, radiation therapy, or investigatorial therapy for any other malignancy within the preceding 12 months. Biomarkers required: (ER+ or PR+) and HER2-. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of inflammatory breast cancer (T4d). Severe cardiac disease including congestive heart failure NYHA class III or higher. Significant pulmonary disease or severe respiratory illness. Uncontrolled infections. Interstitial lung disease or severe dyspnea at rest requiring supplemental oxygen. Renal insufficiency with estimated creatinine clearance <30 mL/min. Major gastrointestinal resections involving stomach or small bowel, Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, or baseline grade ≥2 diarrhea. Active systemic bacterial infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, active fungal infection, or detectable viral infection (human immunodeficiency virus positivity, active hepatitis B or C). Syncope of cardiovascular etiology, pathologic ventricular arrhythmias (ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation), or prior sudden cardiac arrest.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Multiple Myeloma. Histology allowed: Plasma cell malignant neoplasm. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease with measurable disease. Prior treatment required: Proteasome inhibitor **and** Immunomodulatory agent **and** Anti‑CD38 antibody (at least three prior lines total). Prior treatment excluded: Anti‑GPRC5D therapy **or** MMAE‑containing therapy **or** allogeneic stem cell transplant. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants exhibiting clinical signs of central nervous system involvement of multiple myeloma.
Known chronic obstructive pulmonary disease or previous history of interstitial lung disease.
Moderate or severe persistent asthma within the past five years or uncontrolled asthma of any severity.
Severe cardiovascular disease that is not adequately controlled.
History of immunodeficiency disease.
Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 2.
Primary refractory multiple myeloma.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumours (any anatomical site). Histology allowed: Any solid‑tumour histologic subtype. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or locally unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: Documented progression on at least one prior standard anticancer therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Presence of any ONE of the following genomic alterations detected prospectively – inactivating ATM mutation **or** MYC amplification **or** FBXW7 mutation **or** CCNE1 amplification **or** mutation/amplification of a SWI/SNF chromatin‐remodeling gene (specifically ARID1A, PBRM1, SMARCA4, ARID2, ARID1B, SMARCA2, SS18) **or** ALT–associated promotor region disruption involving ATRX or DAXX. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active or progressing untreated brain metastases/leptomeningeal disease deemed to require imminent CNS directed therapy; uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses such as active serious infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmia, or NYHA functional classification worse than II; baseline corrected QT interval (QTcF) greater than 470 milliseconds; grade ≥ 2 persistent toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia, well‑controlled endocrinopathies, or grade ≤ 2 skin toxicity); receipt of any other investigational agent concurrently; pregnancy or nursing motherhood; inability to comply with contraceptive requirements; documented hypersensitivity to components of peposertab or tuvusertib; inability to safely discontinue potent CYP3A4/5, CYP2C19, CYP2C9 modulators, P‑gp/BCRP/OCT1/OATP substrate medicines when medically appropriate; recent (>21 days) major surgical procedure, therapeutic radiation, or immunotherapy (within stipulated windows) — although timeframe specifications are waived for defining trial spaces, they remain exclusionary overall; inadequate organ function outside predefined laboratory thresholds (e.g., hemoglobin < 9 g/dL, ANC < 1500/mm³, platelets < 100 k/µL, total bilirubin > 1.5 × ULN absent liver involvement, AST/ALT exceeding permissible levels, glomerular filtration rate < 60 ml/min/1.73 m²).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non squamous. Cancer burden allowed: Stage IIIB or IIIC unresectable or not candidate for definitive chemoradiation, or Stage IV disease; measurable disease per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior systemic therapy for advanced or metastatic disease; any antibody‑drug conjugate containing a topoisomerase I cytotoxic payload; any tropomyosin receptor kinase‑targeted therapy; any anti‑Programmed Death‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2 therapy; any other immune checkpoint inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Programmed death ligand‑1 tumour proportion score ≥50% (central 22C3 IHC) and provision of tumor tissue for Trop‑2 protein expression assessment. Biomarkers excluded: detectable epidermal growth factor receptor activating mutation, anaplastic lymphoma kinase rearrangement, ros1 rearrangement, ntrk fusion, braf V600E mutation, ret fusion, met exon‑14 skipping alteration, or any other actionable driver alteration with an approved targeted therapy.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, including radiation‑induced forms. Presence of symptomatic or untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; however, patients with previously treated, asymptomatic, stable brain lesions may enroll. Uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions such as recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, prolonged QTc interval, reduced left ventricular ejection fraction, New York Heart Association class II–IV heart failure, or severely elevated blood pressures. Clinically significant corneal or other eye disease. Recent receipt of live or live‑attenuated vaccinations. Active, poorly controlled autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within two years. Known immunodeficiency, ongoing systemic corticosteroids >10 mg/day prednisone equivalents, or other immunosuppressants near baseline. Untreated or inadequately controlled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection, active hepatitis B or C infection, or unresolved infectious processes necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy. Previous solid organ or hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. History of severe hypersensitivity reaction to components of datopotamab deruxtecan or pembrolizumab.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Any solid tumor. Histology allowed: Histologically or cytologically confirmed carcinoma, sarcoma, melanoma, or other malignant neoplasm. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced, unresectable, and/or metastatic disease (measurable disease per RECIST 1.1); asymptomatic or treated central nervous system involvement permitted. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Presence of a KRAS G12C or KRAS G12D or KRAS G12V or KRAS G12A or KRAS G12S or KRAS G13D mutation identified in tumor tissue or circulating tumor DNA (assessment performed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Have known active central nervous system metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis.
Unresolved toxicities from prior therapy greater than National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events version 5.0 Grade 1 at baseline (except alopecia, hearing loss, peripheral neuropathy, and stable endocrine disorders).
Significant cardiovascular disease including unstable angina, acute coronary syndrome, history of myocardial infarction, reduced left‑ventricular ejection fraction or heart failure, or uncontrolled/symptomatic cardiac arrhythmia.
Active hepatitis B virus infection, hepatitis C virus infection, or untreated human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Other concurrent active malignancy unless in complete remission with anticipated survival longer than two years.
Active uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection.
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis / interstitial lung disease requiring steroid therapy or currently clinically significant pneumonitis / interstitial lung disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: HER2‑positive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable or metastatic disease with measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1, including patients with treated or clinically inactive central nervous system metastases. Prior treatment required: prior trastuzumab deruxtecan (patients must have progressed on or be intolerant to it). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: HER2‑positive (IHC 3⁺ or ISH‑amplified) as assessed by central laboratory according to ASCO‑CAP guidelines during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Has known or suspected leptomeningeal disease.; Has uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease.; Has residual toxicity from prior cancer therapy greater than Grade 1.; Requires intravenous antimicrobial therapy for ongoing infection.; Has known human immunodeficiency virus infection.; Has active hepatitis B or C infection.; Has active severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 infection.; Has a history of life‑threatening hypersensitivity reaction to monoclonal antibodies, recombinant proteins, or excipients in zanidatamab.; Unable to receive trastuzumab owing to medical contraindication.; Presence of any serious underlying medical or psychiatric disorder compromising safe participation.; Condition precluding administration of the chosen chemotherapeutic agent.; Any situation deemed by investigators to jeopardize participant safety or compromise data integrity.; History of prior allogeneic bone marrow, stem cell, or solid organ transplantation.; Known hypersensitivity to any component of the study drugs, including chemotherapy.; Pregnant or nursing females, or males/females intending to become pregnant during the study period.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate carcinoma. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma (pure, without neuroendocrine components). Cancer burden allowed: Presence of ≥1 metastatic lesion confirmed by CT/MRI/bone scan within 28 days and documentation of disease progression per PCWG3 criteria (≥2 rising PSA values, soft‑tissue growth ≥20%, or emergence of ≥2 new bone lesions). Prior treatment required: Surgical or chemical castration resulting in castrate testosterone level together with continuous androgen deprivation therapy; documented progression on at least one androgen‑receptor directed therapy (enzalutamide **or** abiraterone **or** apalutamide **or** darolutamide); receipt of exactly one taxane chemotherapy agent in the metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer setting (docetaxel or cabazitaxel, with prior docetaxel exposure permissible only in the hormone‑sensitive stage). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
- Prior steap1‑targeted therapy.
- Ongoing concurrent cytotoxic chemotherapy, androgen‑receptor directed therapy, immunotherapy, radioligand therapy, PARP inhibitor, biological therapy, or investigational therapy.
- History of central nervous system metastasis (treated, asymptomatic, clinically stable dural metastases are exempt).
- Unresolved toxicities from preceding anti‑tumor therapy persisting above grade 1 (exceptions: alopecia or stable, well‑controlled adverse effects).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: oligometastatic disease with 1‑5 PSMA‑positive metastatic lesions on PSMA PET/CT, inclusive of at least one distant metastasis (M1a, M1b, or M1c) per AJCC version 8, and all lesions must be amenable to stereotactic body radiotherapy. Prior treatment required: definitive therapy to the prostate comprising (radical prostatectomy WITH OR WITHOUT postoperative radiation to the prostate bed/pelvic nodes) OR (external beam radiation therapy TO THE PROSTATE ALONE OR INCLUDING SEMINAL VESICLE/PELVIC NODES) OR (brachytherapy), together with documentation of biochemical recurrence satisfying PSA criteria. Prior treatment excluded: any prior radiopharmaceutical agent (e.g., strontium‑89, prior PSMA‑directed radionuclide therapy); any systemic chemotherapy delivered for metastatic prostate cancer; any immunotherapeutic product (e.g., sipuleucel‑T); any poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitor; any investigational systemic agent intended for metastatic disease; chronic androgen deprivation therapy or androgen‑pathway inhibitor therapy continued up to enrolment (regardless of duration); concurrent hormonal agents indicated for prostate cancer such as estrogens, 5‑alpha‑reductase inhibitors (finasteride, dutasteride), or other steroidogenesis inhibitors (aminoglutethimide) when used for prostate cancer management. Biomarkers required: demonstration of PSMA positivity on PET/CT scanning; serum total testosterone >100 ng/dL measured at screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pulmonary toxicity or prior pneumonitis.; Clinically meaningful congestive heart failure or left ventricular ejection fraction markedly reduced.; Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence.; Moderate-to-severe hepatic dysfunction (Child-Pugh class B/C).; Untreated or unstable brain/metastatic central nervous system involvement.; Active human immunodeficiency virus infection or uncontrolled hepatitis B or C viral replication.; Baseline electrocardiographic abnormalities predisposing to serious arrhythmic events (e.g., sustained ventricular tachycardia, second‑degree or higher atrioventricular block without pacing, congenital long QT syndrome, personal or strong family history of torsades de pointes).; Poor functional status (ECOG performance status ≥3).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma or other subtypes classified as non‑small cell lung cancer. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or locally advanced disease; measurable disease per RECIST version 1.1; brain metastases permissible if previously treated, asymptomatic, stable, and not requiring chronic glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisone equivalents daily). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Epidermal growth factor receptor mutation–positive (EGFRmut). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or unresolved/suspected ILD/pneumonitis at screening.
Spinal cord compression or prior/leptomeningeal carcinomatous meningitis.
Active infections including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.
Uncontrolled concurrent serious medical conditions, particularly significant cardiovascular disease as detailed in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma or other subtypes classified as non‑small cell lung cancer. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease; measurable disease per RECIST version 1.1; brain metastases permissible if previously treated, asymptomatic, stable, and not requiring chronic glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisone equivalents daily). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Wild‑type epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFRWT). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or unresolved/suspected ILD/pneumonitis at screening.
Spinal cord compression or prior/leptomeningeal carcinomatous meningitis.
Active infections including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.
Uncontrolled concurrent serious medical conditions, particularly significant cardiovascular disease as detailed in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: head and neck squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity. Histology allowed: Squamous cell carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Recurrent or metastatic disease; measurable disease per RECIST version 1.1; brain metastases permissible if previously treated, asymptomatic, stable, and not requiring chronic glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisone equivalents daily). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or unresolved/suspected ILD/pneumonitis at screening.
Spinal cord compression or prior/leptomeningeal carcinomatous meningitis.
Active infections including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.
Uncontrolled concurrent serious medical conditions, particularly significant cardiovascular disease as detailed in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: colorectal cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease; measurable disease per RECIST version 1.1; brain metastases permissible if previously treated, asymptomatic, stable, and not requiring chronic glucocorticoids (>10 mg prednisone equivalents daily). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, or unresolved/suspected ILD/pneumonitis at screening.
Spinal cord compression or prior/leptomeningeal carcinomatous meningitis.
Active infections including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.
Uncontrolled concurrent serious medical conditions, particularly significant cardiovascular disease as detailed in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: plasma cell neoplasm (multiple myeloma). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease per International Myeloma Working Group criteria with measurable disease defined as serum M‑protein ≥ 0.5 g/dL or urine M‑protein ≥ 200 mg/24 h or involved serum free light chain ≥ 10 mg/dL with abnormal κ/λ ratio. Prior treatment required: ≥ two prior systemic therapy lines and exposure to an immunomodulatory imide drug (e.g., lenalidomide or pomalidomide) and exposure to a proteasome inhibitor and exposure to an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody (e.g., daratumumab). Prior treatment excluded: prior iberdomide exposure or prior belantamab mafodotin exposure or any prior BCMA‑directed therapy or allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status > 2.
Absolute neutrophil count < 1,000/mm³.
Platelet count < 75,000/mm³ (or < 50,000/mm³ in Phase II if bone marrow plasma cells > 50%).
Calculated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min.
Total bilirubin > 2 mg/dL.
Aspartate aminotransferase or alanine aminotransferase > 2.5×upper limit of normal.
Active untreated eye disease or moderate/severe keratopathy.
Live or live‑attenuated vaccines administered within 30 days pre‑registration.
Plasmapheresis performed within 7 days pre‑registration.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (recent MI, unstable angina, symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA III–IV, uncontrolled hypertension, significant arrhythmias, prolonged QTc > 480 ms, left ventricle EF < 30%, recent revascularization procedures within 3 months).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding; inadequate contraception measures for persons of child‑bearing potential.
Significant hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh grade > 1) or cirrhosis.
Active severe infection (bacterial, viral, fungal) at screening.
Concurrent strong CYP3A4 inducer or inhibitor usage unable to be discontinued appropriately.
History of severe allergy to lenalidomide, pomalidomide, or related compounds.
Symptomatic amyloid deposition disorders (amyloidosis, POEMS syndrome) or overt plasma cell leukaemia.
Inadequate organ function preventing safe delivery of study medication (per protocol laboratory cutoffs).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Cutaneous malignant melanoma. Histology allowed: Malignant melanoma. Cancer burden allowed: AJCC stage IIIB, IIIC, IIID, or IV (local‐advanced or metastatic disease, surgically resectable, unresectable, or postoperative). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior immunotherapy for metastatic melanoma except anti‑CTLA‑4 (e.g., prior PD‑1/PD‑L1 therapy for metastatic disease). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of FLT‑3 mutation or amplification.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal metastases.
Active, known or suspected autoimmune disease (exception for vitiligo, Type I diabetes, controlled hypothyroidism, mild psoriasis).
Systemic corticosteroid use greater than 10 mg prednisone equivalents daily or other immunosuppressive medication administered within 14 days of study drug dosing.
Positive laboratory evidence of hepatitis B surface antigen, hepatitis C viral RNA, or HIV infection.
Pregnancy or currently breastfeeding.
History of severe allergic reaction to any vaccine therapy.
Known hypersensitivity to nivolumab, pembrolizumab, or CDX‑301.
Planned major surgery unrelated to melanoma resection.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: multiple myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed disease with measurable disease per IMWG criteria and intent to undergo high‑dose therapy and autologous stem cell transplant (transplant‑eligible). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy directed at any target and any prior B‑cell maturation antigen–targeted therapy and any prior anti‑myeloma therapy other than a short course of corticosteroids. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of central nervous system involvement or clinical signs of meningeal involvement of multiple myeloma.
Stroke or seizure occurring within six months prior to informed consent.
Use of a strong cytochrome P450 3A4 inducer within five half‑lifetimes prior to randomization.
Receipt of or intention to receive any live, attenuated vaccine (other than COVID‑19 vaccines) within four weeks prior to randomization.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: small cell lung carcinoma. Histology allowed: small cell lung carcinoma according to WHO 2021, including combined small cell lung carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: extensive stage or unresectable disease with at least one radiographically measurable lesion per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of standard systemic therapy for small cell lung carcinoma leading to progression or insufficient response. Prior treatment excluded: prior receipt of any cellular immunotherapy (e.g., CAR‑T) or gene therapy product; prior DLL3‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: archived or fresh tumour tissue suitable for laboratory evaluation (formalin‑fixed paraffin‑embedded block or unstained slides) obtained before enrolment. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Checkpoint inhibitor associated pneumonitis  
• Clinically significant ascites, pleural or peritoneal effusions  
• Severe congenital or acquired immunodeficiency lacking controllability  
• Leptomeningeal metastases  
• Active or symptomatic brain metastases (untreated)  
• Autoimmune diseases requiring systemic immunosuppression exceeding physiologic dosing within two weeks prior to screening  
• Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including unstable angina or myocardial infarction within six months, or left ventricular ejection fraction <45%  
• Recent major surgical procedure within four weeks preceding apheresis or anticipated within four weeks post‑treatment  
• Persistent adverse events greater than grade 1 attributable to prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia)  
• Dependence on supplemental oxygen to maintain saturations  
• Untreated active infections (bacterial, viral, fungal) or medically uncontrolled infectious processes  
• Positive HIV infection not adequately suppressed, or hepatitis B/C infection not meeting stipulated management criteria  
• Hypersensitivity or contraindication to study drug constituents (dimethyl sulphoxide, fludarabine, cyclophosphamide, tocilizumab)  
• Pregnancy or breastfeeding, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child within twelve months following infusion  
• Prior allogenic haematopoietic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation  
• Significant cognitive impairment, dementia, or altered mental status compromising participation  
• Other serious or uncontrolled medical conditions judged by investigator to pose undue risk or impede adherence to protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: large cell neuroendocrine lung carcinoma. Histology allowed: large cell neuroendocrine lung carcinoma according to WHO 2021, including combined large cell neuroendocrine lung carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: extensive stage or unresectable disease with at least one radiographically measurable lesion per RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of standard systemic therapy for large cell neuroendocrine lung carcinoma leading to progression or insufficient response. Prior treatment excluded: prior receipt of any cellular immunotherapy (e.g., CAR‑T) or gene therapy product; prior DLL3‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: archived or fresh tumour tissue suitable for laboratory evaluation (formalinfixed paraffin‑filled block or unstained slides) obtained before enrolment. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

• Leptomeningeal metastases  
• Active or symptomatic brain metastases (untreated)

• Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including unstable angina or myocardial infarction within six months, or left ventricular ejection fraction <45%  
• Recent major surgical procedure within four weeks preceding apheresis or anticipated within four weeks post‑treatment  
• Persistent adverse events greater than grade 1 attributable to prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia)  
• Dependence on supplemental oxygen to maintain saturations  
• Untreated active infections (bacterial, viral, fungal) or medically uncontrolled infectious processes  
• Positive HIV infection not adequately suppressed, or hepatitis B/C infection not meeting stipulated management criteria  
• Hypersensitivity or contraindication to study drug constituents (dimethyl sulphoxide, fludarabine, cyclophosphamide, tocilizumab)  
• Pregnancy or breastfeeding, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child within twelve months following infusion  
• Prior allogenic haematopoietic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation  
• Significant cognitive impairment, dementia, or altered mental status compromising participation  
• Other serious or uncontrolled medical conditions judged by investigator to pose undue risk or impede adherence to protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Any. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Epstien‑Barr virus‑associated post‑transplant lymphoproliferative disease. Histology allowed: EBV‑positive post‑transplant lymphoproliferative disease. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable systemic disease evidenced by FDG‑avid lesions (Deauville score ≥ 3) on PET/CT or MRI; untreated central nervous system disease excluded. Prior treatment required: History of solid organ transplant (kidney, liver, heart, lung, pancreas, small intestine, or any combination) and documented failure of rituximab monotherapy (commercial product or biosimilar) for PTLD. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Tumor EBV positivity. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Burkitt lymphoma, classical Hodgkin lymphoma, or any T‑cell lymphoma.; Daily corticosteroids exceeding 0.5 mg/kg prednisolone equivalents, ongoing methotrexate, or extracorporeal photopheresis.; Untreated central nervous system PTLD or active CNS‑directed chemotherapy or radiation at enrollment.; Confirmed grade ≥ 2 graft‑versus‑host disease at enrollment.; Recent exposure to checkpoint inhibitor agents within three drug half‑lives of dosing.; Active adenovirus viremia in allogeneic transplant recipients.; Requirement for vasopressor or mechanical respiratory support.; Administration of antithymocyte globulin or comparable anti‑T‑cell antibodies within four weeks preceding enrollment.; Previous administration of EBV‑targeted cytotoxic T‑cells, CD19‑CAR T‑cells, or unselected donor lymphocyte infusions within eight weeks before enrollment.; Pregnancy, lactation, or lack of adequate contraceptive measures for women of child‑bearing potential or men with partners of child‑bearing potential.; Inability to comply with protocol‑required assessments.; Any severe medical condition or organ dysfunction deemed by the investigators to jeopardize subject safety or study completion.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Any. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Epstien‑Barr virus‑associated post‑transferm lymphoproliferative disease. Histology allowed: EBV‑positive post‑tran​sfert lymphoproliferative disease. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable systemic disease evidenced by FDG‑avid lesions (Deauville score ≥ 3) on PET/CT or MRI; untreated central nervous system disease excluded. Prior treatment required: History of solid organ transplant (kidney, liver, heart, lung, pancreas, small intestine, or any combination) and documented failure of rituximab together with any concurrently or subsequently administered chemotherapy regimen for PTLD. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Tumor EBV positivity. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Any. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Epstien‑Barr virus‑associated post‑transplant lymphoproliferative disease. Histology allowed: EBV‑positive post‑transplant lymphoproliferative disease. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable systemic disease evidenced by FDG‑avid lesions (Deauville score ≥ 3) on PET/CT or MRI; untreated central nervous system disease excluded. Prior treatment required: History of allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplant and documented failure of rituximab monotherapy (commercial product or biosimilar) for PTLD. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Tumor EBV positivity. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic disease with at least one metastatic lesion (bone or soft‑tissue). Prior treatment required: At least one taxane chemotherapy (docetaxel or cabazitaxel) **and** at least one androgen‑receptor pathway inhibitor (abiraterone, enzalutamide, apalutamide or darolutamide). Prior treatment excluded: Previous administration of 177Lu‑PSMA‑617. Biomarkers required: PSMA‑positive lesion with uptake greater than liver on ^68Ga‑PSMA‑11 PET **and** serum testosterone ≤50 ng/dL. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants currently receiving any other investigational agents.
History of allergic reactions attributable to compounds chemically or biologically similar to 177Lu‑PSMA‑617 or carboplatin.
Untreated brain metastases (treated brain metastases acceptable if stable off steroids for ≥4 weeks).
Active leptomeningeal disease.
Symptomatic spinal cord compression or imminent cord compromise.
Unresolved toxicities greater than Grade 1 remaining from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia).
Severe uncontrolled co‑morbidities such as interstitial lung disease, resting dyspnea requiring supplemental oxygen, severe renal insufficiency (estimated CrCL <30 mL/min), gastrointestinal disorders leading to persistent Grade 2 or worse diarrhea, or any other life‑threatening illness judged unsuitable by the investigators.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Synovial sarcoma. Histology allowed: Synovial sarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced (metastatic or unresectable). Prior treatment required: Relapsed from, refractory to, or intolerant to standard‐of‐care therapy for synovial sarcoma.
