clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: all. Cancer type allowed: triple negative breast cancer. Histology allowed: breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: advanced/metastatic or unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: disease refractory to standard therapy or progressive after standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior exposure to BET inhibitor, RAF inhibitor, MEK inhibitor, or ERK inhibitor; prior hormone‐receptor‑positive breast cancer. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor < 1% **and** progesterone receptor < 1% **and** HER2 0‑1+ or non‑amplified. Biomarkers excluded: none specifically noted for this cohort.
general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with persistent toxicity greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (aside from alopecia or peripheral neuropathy). Current receipt of any other investigational agent. Known hypersensitivity to components of ZEN003694 or binimetinib. Requirement for therapeutic‑dose anticoagulation. Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers without appropriate discontinuation. Medications that significantly prolong the QT interval. Uncontrolled intercurrent acute illness. Significant cardiac disease (history of stroke, myocardial infarction, or unstable angina within six months). Active severe hepatic impairment, renal insufficiency, or abnormal coagulation parameters outside permitted ranges. Clinically significant gastrointestinal disorders affecting oral drug absorption (e.g., gastrectomy, bowel resection, chronic diarrhoea, gastric emptying disorder). Retinal vein occlusion history. Past or current pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease. Pregnancy or lactation. Women of childbearing potential or sexually active men unwilling or unable to practice adequate contraceptive measures. Psychiatric or social circumstances compromising adherence to study protocols.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 18 years. Sex allowed: all. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor (any tissue of origin). Histology allowed: NA. clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: any malignancy for which an FDA‑approved epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor is prescribed according to labeled indication. Histology allowed: any. Prior treatment required: current receipt of an epidermal growth factor receptor inhibitor. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe cutaneous toxicity (rash grade = 4 or body‑surface‑area involvement > 30%). Presence of any underlying physical or psychiatric disorder making protocol adherence unlikely. Past or existing dermatologic diseases such as atopic dermatitis, psoriasis, or chronic/recurrent skin infections that could confound evaluation. Baseline laboratory abnormalities: absolute neutrophil count < 1000/mm³, white blood cell count < 3000/mm³, platelet count < 50 000/mm³, alanine aminotransferase > 2.5× upper limit of normal, aspartate aminotransferase > 2.5× upper limit of normal, serum bilirubin > 1.5× upper limit of normal, serum creatinine > 1.5× upper limit of normal. Concurrent planned radiation to head, neck, or upper torso together with EGFR inhibitor therapy or radiation administered within four weeks preceding enrollment. Known hypersensitivity to aprepitant or any component of the investigational product. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during the study period. Uncontrolled active infection (e.g., bacterial pneumonia) or any serious comorbidity judged by the investigator to interfere with study conduct or increase undue risk.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: classical Hodgkin lymphoma. Histology allowed: classical Hodgkin lymphoma (nodular lymphocyte‑predominant subtype excluded). Cancer burden allowed: Measurable disease (at least one FDG‑avid lesion ≥1.5 cm in longest diameter). Prior treatment required: ≤1 line of prior systemic therapy for Hodgkin lymphoma (radiotherapy does not count); patients who received brentuximab vedotin or an anti‑PD‑1/PD‑L1 agent as part of frontline therapy are eligible if they achieved a complete metabolic response and have not relapsed within six months. Prior treatment excluded: More than one line of prior systemic therapy; intended autologous stem cell transplant consolidation; concomitant systemic corticosteroids >10 mg/day prednisone‑equivalent or other immunosuppressive drugs administered within fourteen days of first study dosing. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Live vaccination within thirty days before enrollment; grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy; prior grade ≥ 3 hypersensitivity to brentuximab vedotin or nivolumab; active central nervous system involvement by lymphoma; second malignant neoplasm not in remission for at least three years (exception for non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ cervix, or indolent tumors unlikely to require treatment for two years); ongoing systemic corticosteroid therapy exceeding ten milligrams daily prednisone equivalence or other immunosuppressives within fourteen days (aside from inhaled/topical steroids or adrenal replacement doses without active autoimmune disease); history of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy; congenital or acquired immunodeficiency; active pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease; clinically significant untreated autoimmune disease (certain mild dermatologic or endocrine disorders exempt); active hepatitis B or C infection (eligible only if virologically inactive as defined); uncontrolled HIV infection (eligibility only if viral load undetectable and CD4 ≥200); recent major cardiovascular events—including stroke, transient ischemic attack, unstable angina, myocardial infarction—or congestive heart failure/New York Heart Association Class III‑IV within six months; current pregnancy or lactation; inability to comply with study procedures.
